## PAGE 02828: (2026x) Creating script operations

- page_id: `272735481`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735481/2026x+Creating+script+operations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:16.616+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x) Creating new operations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

69158788

69158801

69158791

Use the script operation, when you need to specify an inline expression in OCL 2.0, as Java binary, or in any of the modeling tool-supported script languages. The modeling tool supports any script language that has a JSR-223-compliant engine.

Adding a script operation directly into a structured expression is the same as defining an operation in the model and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Calling operations from the model' space=''].

To create a script operation

1. Click Create operation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_operation_in_relation_criteria_dialog.png']
2. Click the **Script** icon on the right side of the dialog.
3. If needed, change the operation name.
4. Click the three dots button next to the **Style** option to define the style****for your script operation. The Style option is available only in the **Dependency Criteria** and**Relation Criteria**dialogs.
5. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended languages (Binary or Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, (i.e., green for fast, yellow for medium, and red for slow). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears. If the engine of the language you need is not built into the modeling tool by default, you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool' space='']to the application yourself. Make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled!
6. Type in the Body box. Use the syntax of the selected language.
7. In the **Listeners Configuration** box, type a reference to the java class, which specifies when the result of the script operation must be recalculated. The listeners configuration is not necessary, but using it can improve the performance.
8. Click the three dots button next to the **Results Filter By Type** box and select one or more element types to filter the result of the script operation. 
The script operation is created. Now you can create a new one, where the result of the previously created operation is used as an argument.

69158787

**Related pages**

- Creating new operations
  - Calling operations from the model

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735051 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02829: (2026x) Creating Setters and Getters

- page_id: `272735051`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735051/2026x+Creating+Setters+and+Getters
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:01.025+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

Setters and Getters are common operations that contain almost every [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']. With the help of our modeling tool, **set** and **get** operations for Class attributes can be generated automatically.

To create a setter or a getter

There are two ways of creating a setter or a getter:

1. From the shortcut menu of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering , and then select Create Setters/Getters .
  1. The Select Attributes/Association Ends dialog opens.
2. Add a tagged value "getter/setter for attribute=attribute_name" to the selected Class.

The names of created operations (**setters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public void set + <attribute name> (<attribute type> <attribute name>)*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **setter** will look this way:

- public void setx (int x);

The names of created operations (**getters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public <attribute type> get + <attribute name> ( )*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **getter** will look this way:

- public int getx ();

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']
- Tools

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732174 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02831: (2026x) Creating SOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272732174`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732174/2026x+Creating+SOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:19.753+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SOV-1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create a SOV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SOV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731152 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02832: (2026x) Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `272731152`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731152/2026x+Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:25.917+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.

To create a Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731150 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02833: (2026x) Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow diagram

- page_id: `272731150`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731150/2026x+Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:25.187+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731784 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02834: (2026x) Creating StdV-1 table

- page_id: `272731784`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731784/2026x+Creating+StdV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:40.326+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Standards viewpoint > (2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['create-stdv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-1 table, do either

- Create a new element: 1) Click the Add New button. 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element. 3) In the Select Owner dialog choose the owner and click OK . 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click Close . The element appears under the System element column. 5) Select the cell under the Standard/Policy column and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . 6) In the Select Standard dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.
- Add an existing element. 
 1) Click the**Add Existing** button. 
 2) In the **Select UAF element** dialog select desired elements and click **OK**. The element appears under the **System element** column. 
 3) Select the cell under the **Standard/Policy** column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 4) In the **Select Standard** dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The element appears under the <strong>System element</strong> column.<br /> 5) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .<br /> 6) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.<span> </span></li><li><p>Add an existing element.<br /> 1) Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button.<br /> 2) In the <strong>Select UAF element</strong> dialog select desired elements and click <strong>OK</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731777 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02835: (2026x) Creating StdV-2 table

- page_id: `272731777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731777/2026x+Creating+StdV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:39.934+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Standards viewpoint > (2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['create-stdv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658487619

1658487630

1658487620

To create an StdV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658487617

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="id">1658487620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an StdV-2 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the <strong>&lt;view name&gt;</strong> Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;diagram name&gt;</strong>.</li><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram.<br />The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.</p><h3>Adding rows</h3><p>To add rows with new resources</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong>.</li><li>Choose a resource type from the list.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose the owner.</li><li>In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add rows with existing resources</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Element </strong>dialog, choose a needed resource.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Adding new columns</h3><p>To add new columns</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add / Remove Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Filling cells</h3><p>When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d785b94-7113-40d3-8867-688f0f3fb55b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add a Subject of Forecast</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a needed cell.</li><li>Click the <strong>...</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Subject of Forecast</strong> dialog, choose the resources you need to add.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbc128d0-595e-48fb-ba4b-833cb4c1fcc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.</p><h3>Removing columns</h3><p>To remove columns</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add / Remove Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_columns.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating StdV-2 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Sorting columns</h3><p>After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.</p><p><br /></p><p>To sort columns</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Sort Columns</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d30e4040-0f0c-4e2f-af8d-e0a6316c6489"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1658487617</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732039 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02836: (2026x) Creating structure models by behavior models

- page_id: `272732039`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732039/2026x+Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:09.370+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

If the **Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model** command is not available under **Tools** in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:

- The Allocation Mode is set to Usage ( **Options** >**Project** > **SysML** > **Allocation** Mode ).
- The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click a Block.
  - On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88cdcbe9-295e-4d19-bddc-f507bc82474a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong> command is not available under <strong>Tools</strong> in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Allocation Mode</strong> is set to <strong>Usage </strong>(<strong><strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Project</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong> &gt; <strong>Allocation</strong></strong> <strong>Mode</strong>).</li><li><p>The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click a Block. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating structure models by behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738679 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02837: (2026x) Creating structure models by behavior models in UAF

- page_id: `272738679`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738679/2026x+Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:28.014+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer.
  - On the structure diagram pane, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performershape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the structure diagram pane, right-click an <span>Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer </span>shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating structure models by behavior models in UAF" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram pane. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739201 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02838: (2026x) Creating StV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272739201`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739201/2026x+Creating+StV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:04.104+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.

To create a StV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.</p><p>To create a StV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739199 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02839: (2026x) Creating StV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739199/2026x+Creating+StV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:03.885+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.

To create a StV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property 
*environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.</p><p>To create a StV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property<br /><em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731793 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02840: (2026x) Creating StV-4 diagram

- page_id: `272731793`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731793/2026x+Creating+StV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:41.136+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.

To create a StV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739255 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02841: (2026x) Creating SubChoreography

- page_id: `272739255`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739255/2026x+Creating+SubChoreography
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:47:20.657+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > (2026x) Using BPMN Choreography Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SubChoreography can be displayed with different symbol properties:

- An Expanded SubChoreography can contain inner shapes.
- A Collapsed SubChoreography cannot display inner shapes. A plus sign (+) marker is displayed for a collapsed subprocess.

To create SubChoreography

- On the Choreography diagram pallet, click SubChoreography and select a needed SubChoreography.

To expand a SubChoreography, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu select Suppress Content .
- Right-click a SubChoreography and select Symbol(s) Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens. Select or clear the Suppress Content and click OK .

To collapse a SubChoreography, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu clear the Suppress Content .
- Right-click a SubChoreography and select Symbol(s) Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens. Clear the Suppress Content and click OK .

**Related element**

- SubChoreography

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- Using Choreography Activity
- Creating and Using Choreography Task
- Creating Call Choreography Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A plus sign (+) marker is displayed for a collapsed subprocess.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create SubChoreography</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="00b35a82-e9c5-454f-b64e-6d8631c42f54">On the Choreography diagram pallet, click <strong>SubChoreography</strong> and select a needed SubChoreography.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To expand a SubChoreography, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="dfb88d6b-4c4e-4f12-98fc-4a87d56d681d">Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu select <strong>Suppress Content</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="b0f88e98-7443-4929-9262-0a262cf18a45">Right-click a SubChoreography and select <strong>Symbol(s) Properties</strong>. The <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens. Select or clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong> and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To collapse a SubChoreography, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="aefd04fb-d156-45c2-8545-b14acd0e33e7">Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="315a1b5c-25c0-40b3-b23f-10e9b33baec8">Right-click a SubChoreography and select <strong>Symbol(s) Properties</strong>. The<strong> Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens. Clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong> and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="1b22c6de-55b8-420f-a91f-1107319514d7"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) SubChoreography">SubChoreography</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9d08be4c-d038-41b2-a69b-07948812d3e8"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="82446b0c-d77d-470a-a311-509c259966aa"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e003abde-988b-4b8a-902a-a1d5eeb8e3b4"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+Choreography+Activity">Using Choreography Activity</a></li><li data-uuid="1db2a921-6159-46cd-a5d8-304921509aea"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Creating+and+Using+Choreography+Task">Creating and Using Choreography Task</a></li><li data-uuid="ff473470-3b7e-4cfa-a889-571260f6858e"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Creating+Call+Choreography+Activity">Creating Call Choreography Activity</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739990 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02842: (2026x) Creating SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272739990`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739990/2026x+Creating+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:18.840+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources.</li><li>Connect resources using Association.</li><li>Create Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>flowing via<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Associations.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services.</span> You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739997 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02843: (2026x) Creating SV-1 Internal diagram

- page_id: `272739997`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739997/2026x+Creating+SV-1+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:19.967+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space='']OV-2 Internal diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> Internal diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p> You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731756 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02844: (2026x) Creating SV-10a table

- page_id: `272731756`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731756/2026x+Creating+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:37.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-10a Systems Rules Model
- labels: ['add-resource-constraint-in-sv-10a', 'create-sv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the **Select Subject Of Resource Constraint** dialog choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange.
- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog choose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource Artifact,</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange</span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739921 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02845: (2026x) Creating SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739921`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739921/2026x+Creating+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:13.356+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create resources or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] .
2. Connect resources using Associations.
3. Create Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports. The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating SV-2.</p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources or reuse them from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect resources using Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li><p>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d06663-14f6-438b-8ed6-a66214266957"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link></span></span> diagram.</span></p>
````

- page_id: `272739929`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739929/2026x+Creating+SV-2+Internal+diagram

- labels: ['create-sv-2-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p>You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2 System Internal Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731664 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02847: (2026x) Creating SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272731664`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731664/2026x+Creating+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:32.189+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-3-matrix', 'build-sv-3-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) shows how systems interact to each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource Exchangesand their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource Exchangebetween resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the **Row Scope** (Resources).
2. Specify the **Column Scope** (Resources).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how systems interact to each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource <span>Exchanges </span>and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p>To create a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739203 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02848: (2026x) Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `272739203`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739203/2026x+Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:04.304+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.
5. Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739259 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02849: (2026x) Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

- page_id: `272739259`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739259/2026x+Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:09.647+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731672 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02850: (2026x) Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `272731672`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731672/2026x+Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:32.480+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''].
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731670 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02851: (2026x) Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `272731670`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731670/2026x+Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:32.413+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-flow-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Create Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b36e701-10bd-41e6-a4db-1527fbf1fbf7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Systems Functionality Description diagram</span> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Create <span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchanges</span> </span> on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731677 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02852: (2026x) Creating SV-5a matrix

- page_id: `272731677`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731677/2026x+Creating+SV-5a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:32.663+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5a-matrix', 'build-sv-5a-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5a-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731692 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02853: (2026x) Creating SV-5b matrix

- page_id: `272731692`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731692/2026x+Creating+SV-5b+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:33.040+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5b-matrix', 'build-sv-5b-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5b-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) describes thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space='']) mapping between the Systems and Operational Activities.

The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent resourcesand the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5b matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resources Artifacts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) <span>describes the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) m</span><span>apping between the Systems and Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">resources </span>and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5b matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> (Resources Artifacts).</span></li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731686 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02854: (2026x) Creating SV-6 matrix

- page_id: `272731686`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731686/2026x+Creating+SV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:32.874+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-6', 'create-sv-6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Resource Exchanges, Commands, and Controlsto fill in an SV-6 matrix.

To add new Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges type are added to the matrix.

To add an existing Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchange types are added to the matrix.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges, Commands, and Controls </span>to fill in an SV-6 matrix.</p><p> </p><p>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges type </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>+</strong> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:<br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange types </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731699 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02855: (2026x) Creating SV-8 diagram

- page_id: `272731699`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731699/2026x+Creating+SV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:33.501+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a821551c-c5dd-447f-a321-7fe7999c5434"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3 class="confluence-link">Create new Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Version of Configuration.</li><li>Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.</li></ol><h3>Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select <strong>Resource</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Assignment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="version_of_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731762 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02856: (2026x) Creating SV-9 table

- page_id: `272731762`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731762/2026x+Creating+SV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:38.675+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-sv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658334558

1658334569

1658334559

To create a SV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding new subjects of forecast

To add new subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

##### Adding existing subjects of forecast

To add existing subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual **Strategic**Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658334556

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739807 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02857: (2026x) Creating SvcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272739807`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739807/2026x+Creating+SvcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:59.768+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] views.

To create a SvcV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Specification.
2. Associate Service Specification usingAssociation relationships.

The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Specifications mapping and Service Specificationsinteractions with the system resources.You can model each Service Specifications internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
