/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span> using<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Association relationships</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(51,51,51);" /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s mapping and Service Specifications </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">interactions with the system resources. </span>You can model each <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s</span> internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.</p><p> </p>

- page_id: `272739877`

- labels: ['create-svcv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SvcV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraints. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, DataElement, Resource Exchange).
- Click the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraint from the model.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739897 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02859: (2026x) Creating SvcV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739897`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739897/2026x+Creating+SvcV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:11.267+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] views.

To create a SvcV-2 diagram

1. Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.
2. Associate Service Specifications using Associations .
3. Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.

You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.</li><li>Associate Service Specifications using Associations<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.</span><span> </span></li></ol><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.</p><p>You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Service Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 02861: (2026x) Creating SvcV-3a matrix

- page_id: `272739899`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739899/2026x+Creating+SvcV-3a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:11.493+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-3a-matrix', 'build-svcv-3a-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-3a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

SvcV-3a diagram is non editable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a SvcV-3a matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e189cc52-3a56-4d2b-9975-4242057ac736"><ac:rich-text-body><p>SvcV-3a diagram is non editable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

To create a SvcV-3b matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope ( Service ).
2. Specify the Column Scope ( Service ).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739903 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02863: (2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `272739903`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739903/2026x+Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:11.896+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications.
3. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.
4. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Service Functions.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications<span class="confluence-link">.  </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service  Functions and Operational Activities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739862 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02864: (2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `272739862`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739862/2026x+Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:06.736+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-flow-description', 'display-resource-interactions-in-svcv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='']SvcV-4 Service Functionality Descriptiondiagram should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Control Flow or Service Object Flow relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram" /><ac:link-body>SvcV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Service Functionality Description</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> diagram</span> should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service <span>Control Flow or Service <span> </span>Object Flow relationships.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739867 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02865: (2026x) Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `272739867`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739867/2026x+Creating+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:07.895+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category******Security, Resources,**or **Operational**, and select the desired resource. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource, type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone properties defined:

- Version Released At Milestone (defines the start of a period, Kind - InService) .
- Version Withdrawn At Milestone (defines the end of a period, Kind - OutOfService).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, specify: 
 - the**Version Released At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following: - the **Version Withdrawn At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following:
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind In Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "").
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind Out Of Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "").
2. Click **Close** > **OK**. The Actual Project Milestone is specified.

In_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind In Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the start of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: In Service.
5. Click Close > OK .

Out_Of_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind Out Of Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties:
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the end of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: Out Of Service. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click Close > OK .

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739864 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02866: (2026x) Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `272739864`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739864/2026x+Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:07.590+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658435249

1658435261

1658435250

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strateic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658435247

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739648 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02867: (2026x) Creating SysML v1 projects

- page_id: `272739648`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739648/2026x+Creating+SysML+v1+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:38.792+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are three ways to create the SysML projects.

##### Creating the blank SysML v1 project

To create a blank SysML v1 project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**> **New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select **SysML****v1 Project**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The blank project is created and stored in your file system with a defined name.

##### Creating a SysML v1 project from a template

To create a SysML v1 project from a template

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Other**, select **Project from Template**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Select the **SysML v1** template.
5. Click **OK** when you are done. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating MagicGrid projects

To create a MagicGrid project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select either:
  1. **MagicGrid QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid approach.
  2. **MagicGrid Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid approach.
  3. **MagicGrid v2 QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid v2 approach.
  4. **MagicGrid v2 Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid v2 approach.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. When youcreatea new MagicGrid project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking **Yes**changes the modeling tool's perspective to the MagicGrid perspective. The MagicGrid project is created and stored in your file system with a defined name.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731159 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02868: (2026x) Creating the SV-10a table

- page_id: `272731159`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731159/2026x+Creating+the+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:28.043+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Create New Resource Constraint button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Resource Constraint button and selecting Resource Constraints .

- You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Resource Constraint</strong> button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing Resource Constraint</strong> button and selecting<strong> Resource Constraints</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7381a5-8071-43e0-aad0-e07e2453bdfc"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

2. Choose a resource type from the list.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658644893

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736843 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02870: (2026x) Creating traceability relations

- page_id: `272736843`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736843/2026x+Creating+traceability+relations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:06.623+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1963513353

1963513355

1963513354

Multiple relationships can be used for traceability representation, depending on the element type and method used.

The traceability relations can be represented by the following types of element dependencies:

- UML relationships (such as abstraction, realization, derivation)
- UML properties (such as Owned Behavior )
- UML tags (such as Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams )

The traceability relations can be single level and multilevel.

- When elements are related directly by using a custom UML relationship, property, or tag, the traceability relation is considered to be a single level relation.
- When elements are related indirectly by usually using multiple types of the above mentioned dependencies, the traceability relation is considered to be a multilevel relation.

The traceability relations can be specifying, realizing, or other. This depends on which direction a relation is analyzed and which element is considered as a basis.

- A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the realization of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be realizing traceability (for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Forward traceability - realization' space='']).
- A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the specification of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be specifying traceability (for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Backward traceability – specification' space='']).
- All [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Other traceability relations' space=''].[IMAGE alt='' src='']Horizontal and vertical traceability representation through the realizing, specifying, and other traceability relations

1963513351

**Related Pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739269 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02871: (2026x) Creating TV-1 table

- page_id: `272739269`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739269/2026x+Creating+TV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:10.729+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a TV-1 table, do either

- Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the Add new UPDM Element button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.
- Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the Add Existing UPDM Element button and select the UPDM elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the … button on any cell in the Standard/Policy column.

- You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a TV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the <strong>Add new UPDM Element</strong> button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.</li><li>Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the <strong>Add Existing UPDM</strong> Element button and select the UPDM elements.</li><li>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the<strong> …</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac771b7b-bc23-4d1e-92ac-95be2bc7b64d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731398 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02872: (2026x) Creating TV-2 table

- page_id: `272731398`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731398/2026x+Creating+TV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:51.494+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658721791

1658721806

1658721795

To create a TV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658721790

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738636 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02874: (2026x) Creating validation suites

- page_id: `272738636`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738636/2026x+Creating+validation+suites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:24.877+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Validation > (2026x) Custom validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Additional information

INLINE

The validation suite defines the set of validation rules applied when running the validation. The purpose of the validation suites is to group validation rules (constraints) without duplicating them. When creating a new validation suite you must choose the active or inactive validation suite you want to create, depending on the applied stereotype:

- «validationSuite» is dedicated for inactive validation rules. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Predefined validation suites' space='']>]]>
- «activeValidationSuite» is dedicated for active validation rules. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Active validation suites' space='']>]]>

You can:

- 
- 
- 

#### INFO: Sharing

Sharing

You can share validation suites by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exporting packages to new projects' space=''] and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing used projects' space='']

createTo create a new validation suite

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. Do the one of the following: - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_new_validation_suite.png'] and select Validation Suite or Active Validation Suite command. - Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''] and select Create Validation Suite or Create Active Validation Suite command.
3. In the Select Owner dialog, choose the owner for the new validation suite Package, and click OK .
4. In the **Rename** dialog, type the name of the new validation suite Package, and click **OK**. 
An empty validation suite Package is created and is located in your model. You can include validation rules from other validation suites. >]]> AlternativeAn alternative way is to create a new or select existing Package element and apply one of the following stereotypes:- «validationSuite» 
 - «activeValidationSuite»[How to apply a stereotype >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype)

cloneTo clone existing validation suites

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select the validation suite Package you want to modify.
3. Do the one of the following: 
 - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 - Right-click it and select **Clone Validation Suite** command.
4. In the Select Owner dialog, choose the owner for the cloned validation suite Package, and click OK .
5. In the **Rename** dialog, type the name of the cloned validation suite Package, and click **OK**. 
The clone of the existing validation suite Package is created and contains the same validation rules as the original. You can include or exclude validation rules from the cloned validation suites Package. >]]>

fillTo include/exclude the validation rules to/from a validation suite Package

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select the newly created or cloned validation suite Package. You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select/clear the check boxes for the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule to include/exclude validation rules to/from the new validation suites Package. 
The validation suites Package contains only selected validation rules. Additional informationAfter you include/exclude the validation rules (constraints), the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED'] relation is created/deleted in the model. You can check that in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''], under newly created or cloned validation suites Package, **Relations** group. In the image below, the *Numbering Validation*, *Path Overlapping*, and *Pins Displaying* validation suites with all Constraints are included in the newly created *Specific constraints* validation suite via the Package Import relation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The purpose of the validation suites is to group validation rules (constraints) without duplicating them. ## PAGE 02876: (2026x) CSV Export Report Designer

- page_id: `272730995`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730995/2026x+CSV+Export+Report+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:54.467+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this section, you will learn how to export a Diagram Table in CSV format.

To export a Diagram Table in the CSV format using the Magic Report plugin

1. On the main menu, click Options>Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Report Wizard option group, click Add to add a new row in the Configure template mappings table. [ATTACHMENT filename='Environment Options dialog.png']
3. In the Template mappings dialog, add . csv as the file extension and . txt as the Template type. [ATTACHMENT filename='Template mapping dialog.png']
4. Click OK .
5. Create a blank text file and copy the sample code below in the text file. = 0 || \$value.indexOf(',') >= 0)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#else
#foreach ($c in $value.toCharArray())
#if($c.getType($c)==15)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#break
#end
#end
#end
#end
#import("table", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DiagramTableTool")
#foreach($d in $table.filterDiagramTables($Diagram))
#set($colIds = $table.getColumnIds($d))$d.getName()
#foreach($te in $table.getColumnNames($d, $colIds))#formatValue($te)$value,#end
#foreach($re in $table.getRows($d))
#foreach($id in $colIds)#formatValue($table.getStringCellValue($d, $re, $id))$value,#end
#end
$table.clearTablesCache()
#end]]>
6. Save this text file with a CSV file extension.
7. Add the CSV template to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard dialog' space=''] and generate a CSV report.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this section, you will learn how to export a Diagram Table in CSV format.</p><p>To export a Diagram Table in the CSV format using the Magic Report plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options&gt;Environment</strong>. The Environment Options dialog opens.</li><li>In the Report Wizard option group, click <strong>Add</strong> to add a new row in the Configure template mappings table.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Environment Options dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CSV Export Report Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the Template mappings dialog, add .<strong>csv </strong>as the file extension and .<strong>txt </strong>as the Template type.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Template mapping dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CSV Export Report Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Create a blank text file and copy the sample code below in the text file.<br /><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7c0570a-351b-45ba-b332-f2e8b9b49af3"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#macro(formatValue $s)
#set($value = $s)
#if($value.indexOf('"') >= 0 || \$value.indexOf(',') >= 0)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#else
#foreach ($c in $value.toCharArray())
#if($c.getType($c)==15)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#break
#end
#end
#end
#end
#import("table", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DiagramTableTool")
#foreach($d in $table.filterDiagramTables($Diagram))
#set($colIds = $table.getColumnIds($d))$d.getName()
#foreach($te in $table.getColumnNames($d, $colIds))#formatValue($te)$value,#end
#foreach($re in $table.getRows($d))
#foreach($id in $colIds)#formatValue($table.getStringCellValue($d, $re, $id))$value,#end
#end
$table.clearTablesCache()
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save this text file with a CSV file extension.</li><li>Add the CSV template to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard dialog" /></ac:link> and generate a CSV report.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738960 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02877: (2026x) Custom tool

- page_id: `272738960`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738960/2026x+Custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:53.015+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Custom tool is a template library written in Java to encapsulate functions in context. The Custom tool, see figure below, is extended from the report API. The tool can be used to develop custom context properties that are presentation centric or that can take advantage of the existing ‘tool’ extensions or by document authors familiar with Java.

Report Wizard uses the word 'tool' meaning an object encapsulating dynamic functions on the JavaBean. A tool is a "carrier" of data between the Java and template layers. In other words, the tool simplifies template development and maintenance.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Custom tool API Structure.

#### Context Name

A Context name is a name for variables or references in the template. Report Wizard uses the Velocity Template Language (*VTL*) as a standard syntax for the template. *VTL* uses “$” as a leading character followed by an identifier for the variable.

For example:

```text

```

#### Context Object

A Context object is a Java Object representing a variable. A Context object is modeled on the JavaBean specifications defined by Sun Microsystems.

For example, if a String represents the variable $foo.name in context, the value of the String will be printed out in the output report as follows.

```text

```

A tool is a &quot;carrier&quot; of data between the Java and template layers. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736992 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02878: (2026x) Custom traceability rules

- page_id: `272736992`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736992/2026x+Custom+traceability+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:10.997+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create custom traceability rules if predefined traceability rules are not sufficient to trace and analyze relationships between elements in your model. In a model, custom traceability rules are considered as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='UPDG'], because their values are produced or calculated from other properties. Usually, the values of custom traceability rules cannot be changed directly, but you can and these rules the same way as any other property.

##### Creating a custom traceability rule

Custom traceability rules can be quickly created from the Specification window of a particular element. This way you create a new Derived Property for all elements of the selected element type.

The customization of the newly created traceability rule is saved in the *Derived Properties* Package with the «derivedPropertiesSuite» stereotype. This folder is normally hidden in the containment tree of your model. You can find it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Quick Find dialog' space='']**Quick Find** functionality. This may be useful when you want to change traceability rule properties.

To create a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element for which you want to create a custom traceability rule to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. Click the Create button. [ATTACHMENT filename='1_create_custom_traceability_rule_1.png']
4. In the open Expression dialog, specify the name and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] of a new traceability rule.
5. Click OK and close the Specification window. If you want to create a Derived Property with a result other than an element, you need to change the Derived Property type manually. [ATTACHMENT filename='1_create_custom_traceability_rule_2.png']

To create a custom traceability rule in a table

- In the toolbar of an active table, click the Columns button, and in the menu select the New Derived Property command. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_derived_property_in_a_table.png']

##### Specifying a custom traceability rule

When you create a new custom traceability rule for an element or a Derived Property in a table, you need to define the expression in the **Expression** dialog. Keep in mind:

- The Derived Property type and multiplicity (conveyed via the Single Value check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications in the expression itself. [ATTACHMENT filename='derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png']
- The root union of a Derived Property structured expression has the Unique check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result. By default, the check box is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='unique_ordered_options.png']

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

To learn more about specifying expressions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] page.

##### editEditing a custom traceability rule

You can modify a custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element.

#### NOTE: Editing traceability rule values

Editing traceability rule values

You can edit the value of a custom traceability rule directly only when its operation type is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Simple Navigation' space=''] and you specified only one criterion.

To edit a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to edit to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to edit.
4. Click the Edit button. [ATTACHMENT filename='2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_1.png']
5. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .
6. Close the Specification window.

##### editEditing a custom traceability rule in a table

If a custom traceability rule is displayed in a table, you can edit it directly from that table.

To edit a custom traceability rule in a table

1. In a table, right-click the header of the desired custom traceability rule column and select Edit Derived Property . [ATTACHMENT filename='2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png']
2. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .

##### displayDisplaying a custom traceability rule

If you want to visualize custom traceability rules, you can display them in the compartments of element shapes or in tables.

To display a custom traceability rule on an element shape

1. Do one of the following:
  - In a diagram, right-click the desired element shape and select Edit Compartments .
  - In a diagram, select the desired element shape and click the **Compartments** smart manipulator, click on the down arrow to expand the menu, and select **Edit Compartments**.
2. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab.
3. In the Hidden list on the left side of the dialog, select the traceability rules you want to display.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_to_selected.png'] to add the selected traceability rules to the Selected list on the right side of the dialog. If you want to display all properties from the Hidden list, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_all.png'] .
5. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_element_shape.png']

To display a custom traceability rule in a table

1. In the toolbar of an active table, click the Show Columns button and from the menu select the Select Columns command. [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_1.png']
2. In the Select Columns dialog, set the traceability rules you want to display as true .
3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_2.png']

##### Removing a custom traceability rule

You can remove a Custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element or directly from a table if the traceability rule is displayed in it.

To remove a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to remove to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to remove.
4. Click the Delete button and close the Specification window. [ATTACHMENT filename='4_remove_custom_traceability_rule.png']

To remove a custom traceability rule in a table

- In a table, right-click the header of the unwanted custom traceability rule column and select Remove Derived Property . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_remove_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png']

**Related pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create custom traceability rules if predefined traceability rules are not sufficient to trace and analyze relationships between elements in your model. In a model, custom traceability rules are considered as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derived Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, because their values are produced or calculated from other properties. Usually, the values of custom traceability rules cannot be changed directly, but you can <ac:link ac:anchor="edit" /> and <ac:link ac:anchor="display" /> these rules the same way as any other property.</p><h3>Creating a custom traceability rule</h3><p>Custom traceability rules can be quickly created from the Specification window of a particular element. This way you create a new Derived Property for all elements of the selected element type.</p><p>The customization of the newly created traceability rule is saved in the <em>Derived Properties</em> Package with the «derivedPropertiesSuite» stereotype. This folder is normally hidden in the containment tree of your model. You can find it using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Quick Find dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Quick Find</strong> functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link>. This may be useful when you want to change traceability rule properties.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom traceability rule</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2a2a008d-7796-49af-8534-c53ff86a861a">In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element for which you want to create a custom traceability rule to open its Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="3e48b1b0-9cf5-4c3e-a2b0-2ebe4eeb8d47">In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Traceability</strong> group.</li><li data-uuid="22c899e6-9382-40db-822c-f9e0ba9e8c71">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_custom_traceability_rule_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom traceability rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="914d1530-b42d-454c-b21c-771015b16ddd">In the open <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, specify the name and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a new traceability rule.</li><li data-uuid="59533d51-d0cc-4c47-a25d-d7e89860e01f">Click <strong>OK</strong> and close the Specification window. If you want to create a Derived Property with a result other than an element, you need to change the Derived Property type manually.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_custom_traceability_rule_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom traceability rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom traceability rule in a table</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="5d695d76-0912-483e-9b15-479979774bfd">In the toolbar of an active table, click the <strong>Columns</strong> button, and in the menu select the <strong>New Derived Property</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_derived_property_in_a_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom traceability rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Specifying a custom traceability rule</h3><p>When you create a new custom traceability rule for an element or a Derived Property in a table, you need to define the expression in the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog. Keep in mind:</p><ul><li data-uuid="422a86b0-be57-4e47-8510-45ea87d25db1">The Derived Property <strong>type</strong> and <strong>multiplicity</strong> (conveyed via the <strong>Single Value</strong> check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications in the expression itself.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom traceability rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="ea6a401e-7bbf-425f-8682-168530b84a3b">The root union of a Derived Property structured expression has the <strong>Unique</strong> check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result. By default, the check box is selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="unique_ordered_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom traceability rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62275b61-1db5-4461-b210-61eb3e15d88c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about specifying expressions, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93bf5c32-88d1-4c06-8c19-7f19c09e08af"><ac:parameter to visualize custom traceability rules, you can display them in the compartments of element shapes or in tables.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display a custom traceability rule on an element shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a60b618a-41c5-4a31-a0a8-889f6ed17e84">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In a diagram, right-click the desired element shape and select <strong>Edit select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8f651dd7-8eb6-414f-9cdf-31679ecbc3e4"><p>In the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Element Find an appropriate topic:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737958 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 02880: (2026x) Customize Perspectives dialog

- page_id: `272737958`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737958/2026x+Customize+Perspectives+dialog
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:24:28.744+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the Customize Perspectives dialog

- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**and then **Customize**. falsemcse,msa[IMAGE alt='' src=''] falsemsosa[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Clone SelectedPerspective | Copy the selected perspective to a new one. |
| Rename SelectedPerspective | The Enter Perspective Name dialog opens. Change name of the perspective and click OK. You can also rename perspectives by pressing F2. |
| Remove SelectedPerspective | Delete the selected perspective from the list. |
| Import NewPerspective | The Open dialog appears. Select *.umd extension file and click Open to import the perspective into the modeling tool environment. |
| Export SelectedPerspective | The Save dialog opens. Type a name for the created perspective and click Save to store it as *.umd extension file. |
| Full FeaturedQuick StartSoftware ArchitectSystem Analyst | List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration. |
| MagicDraw Area | List of customizable toolbars and command sections. |
| Edit | Click the Edit button to open the Customize Main Menu dialog.Change he commands mode by selecting radio buttons. |
| Description | Learn the short description about each selected area. |
| Reset to Defaults | Reset all changes back to the default configuration. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_new_perspective.png" /></ac:image></strong><br /></span></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The <strong>Open </strong>dialog appears. Select <em>*.umd</em> extension file <br />and click <strong>Open </strong>to import the perspective into the <br />modeling tool environment.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Export Selected</strong><br /><strong>Perspective <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_selected_perspective.png" /></ac:image></strong><br /></span></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Type a name for the <br />created perspective and click <strong>Save </strong>to store it <br />as <em>*.umd</em> extension file.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Full Featured</strong><br /><strong>Quick Start</strong><br /><strong>Software Architect</strong><br /><strong>System Analyst</strong><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>MagicDraw Area</strong><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">List of customizable toolbars and command sections.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Edit</strong><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Click the <strong>Edit </strong>button to open the <strong>Customize Main Menu </strong>dialog.<br />Change he commands mode by selecting radio buttons.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Description</strong><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Learn the short description about each selected area.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Reset to Defaults</strong><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Reset all changes back to the default configuration.<br /></span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737924 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02881: (2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective

- page_id: `272737924`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737924/2026x+Customizing+and+Selecting+Perspective
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:23.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

278424292

278424294

278424293

The Startup dialog appears when you launch a modeling tool (MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, or Cameo Enterprise Architecture) for the first time. Select your work perspective in this dialog.

Due to their growing numbers, many features may be configured for the standard or expert user. The tool better satisfies the needs of different software development process roles by using Perspective.

Use perspectives for:

- Selecting a predefined configuration and features according to your software development process role.
- Finding features faster, because there are fewer.
- Choosing a suitable experience mode with a single click.
- Customizing a set of predefined features and configurations based on user needs.

There are four perspectives:

- **Full Featured**perspective provides all features available in the modeling tool and installed plugins.
- **Quick Start**perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling with an interface that is not overcrowded, making learning quicker. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden yet easily reachable in expert mode.
- **Software Architect**perspective provides features primarily involved in designing and implementing projects. It is a set of roles consisting of Software Architect, Designer, Interface designer, and Database designer. This is the default modeling tool configuration. All functionalities are available for expert mode.
- **System Analyst**perspective provides features primarily dedicated to obtaining requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Configuration is modeling-oriented. Code engineering, transformations, and other features are hidden.

To set the perspective for the modeling tool environment

- Launch the program for the first time. The **Startup**dialog opens where you canswitch between perspectives. Select the desired perspective from the list and click **OK**.
- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**> **Perspectives**. The **Select****Perspective**dialog opens. Select the desired perspective and click the **Apply**button.
- On the **Perspectives**toolbar, select the desired perspective from the list.

278424290

**Related pages**

true
