<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731091 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02934: (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports

- page_id: `272731091`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731091/2026x+Defining+interfaces+using+ports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:13.587+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['conjugate', 'is_conjugated', 'interface', 'ports', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Ports are points at which external entities can connect to and interact with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] in different or more limited ways than connecting directly to the Block itself. They are properties with a type that specifies features available to the external entities via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] to the ports. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] define the boundary by specifying which features of the owning [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] are visible through external connectors, while [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'] define the boundary with their own features. Proxy Ports are always typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'], a specialized kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] that has no behaviors or internal parts. Full Ports cannot be behavioral in the UML sense of standing in for the owning object, because they handle features themselves, rather than exposing features of their owners, or internal parts of their owners.

In the following figure, the *chanel2* Proxy Port is typed by *GrayCodeIB* Interface Block which contains two Flow Properties of *Pulse* Signal:*out a* and *out b*. It means the Proxy Port that is typed by *GrayCodeIB* Interface Block can send and receive Flow Items of *Pulse* Signal.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The GrayCodeIB Interface Block is a type of Proxy Ports.

To define interfaces using ports

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] and decompose it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining Blocks in Block Definition Diagram' space='']>]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Decomposing Blocks' space='']>]]>
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']SysML Internal Block Diagram , create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating ports' space='']>]]>
3. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] in the model and specify its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining Interface Blocks' space='']>]]>
4. Select create Interface Block as Proxy Port type. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting port type' space='']>]]>

If ports are connected via a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'], both port types must be compatible.

If the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] is set as the type for ports on both ends, one of the ports should be conjugated. The conjugate mechanism reverses the direction of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] and makes the connection compatible. For this, you can conjugate a port or create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conjugated Interface Block' space='MED'].

To conjugate a port, do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, right-click the port shape, and select the Is Conjugated command.
- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] of port, and set the Is Conjugated property value to true. The port is conjugated and the tilde symbol (~) appears before the port's type. See the following figure. [ATTACHMENT filename='conjugated_port.png']

To create a Conjugated Interface Block

- Right-click one or more Interface Blocks and select Tools > Create Conjugated Interface Block . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conjugated Interface Block' space='MED'] is created with the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] as the original but with opposite directions. It is created under the same owner as the original Interface Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figure of this page is the **Inverted****Pendulum** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='InvertedPendulum.mdzip'].
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Inverted Pendulum\Inverted Pendulum.mdzip.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734205 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02935: (2026x) Defining layout template

- page_id: `272734205`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734205/2026x+Defining+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:30.124+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880485

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880487

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880486

If you want to reuse the style and layout of Class with its Ports in another diagram, you need to define the appearance of the Class, the style, and position of its Ports in the Class diagram. It means that you define a new layout template. When defining the appearance of the Class, you can change its color, font, size, layout, and other style properties. To do this, follow the procedure below.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

To define the layout template

1. Create the Class diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. Create a new or represent an existing Class with its Ports on the diagram pane.
3. Specify the appearance of all shapes by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']Symbol Properties dialog and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] .
4. Set the positions of Ports on Class shape manually. 
The appearance of Class and its Ports shapes are defined (see the figure below). [IMAGE alt='' src='']The comparison of default and defined appearance of the Modem Card Block, style, and the position of its ports. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

#### INFO: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

- From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.
- If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating layout template' space=''] directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.

57880484

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is the **Modem Cable** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It means that you define a new layout template. When defining the appearance of the Class, you can change its color, font, size, layout, and other style properties. To do this, follow the procedure below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8579265-ff01-4631-a7bb-2ec607d5bbd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To define the layout template</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the Class diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create diagrams >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Create a new or represent an existing Class with its Ports on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify the appearance of all shapes by using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Symbol Properties</span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><p>Set the positions of Ports on Class shape manually.<br />The appearance of Class and its Ports shapes are defined (see the figure below).<br /><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparison_of_default_and_user_defined_appearance_of_block_and_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Defining layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>The comparison of default and defined appearance of the Modem Card Block, style, and the position of its ports. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="136bf21e-c771-40e3-b5f7-62d0f1b41818"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.</li><li>If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a layout template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2701dce5-9e6b-496d-8490-1d529de1b398"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">57880484</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is the <strong>Modem Cable</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736828 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02936: (2026x) Defining properties of the customized element to be spell checked

- page_id: `272736828`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736828/2026x+Defining+properties+of+the+customized+element+to+be+spell+checked
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:49.211+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Spelling Checker
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check both DSL customization classes and their properties. You can choose which properties of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space='UPDG'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']with a «Customization» [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']) to be spell checked.

You accomplish this by defining a customization class [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MED']*checkSpelling* value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MED']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Spelling Checker' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can check both DSL customization classes and their properties. You can choose which properties of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Customization Elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customized element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with a «Customization» <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) to be spell checked.</p><p>You accomplish this by defining a customization class <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tag ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em>checkSpelling</em> value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="8846de97-d471-4c9d-8adc-5f6433e15445"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75f757a6-f50b-4e54-a1ca-bbeb27b839bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738977 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02937: (2026x) Definitions

- page_id: `272738977`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738977/2026x+Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:53.459+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) Common BPMN Elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Definitions element is a root model in a business modeling project. This element is the outermost containing object for all BPMN elements. It defines the visibility scope and the namespace for all of the BPMN elements in a model.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Definitions element in Containment tree

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Definitions element is a root model in a business modeling project. This element is the outermost containing object for all BPMN elements. It defines the visibility scope and the namespace for all of the BPMN elements in a model.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Definitions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>Definitions element in Containment tree</h6><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731444 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02938: (2026x) Deleting actual projects from NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731444`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731444/2026x+Deleting+actual+projects+from+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:57.586+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete an actual project from the chart

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete From Chart**. Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.

To delete an actual project from the model

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To delete an actual project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete From Chart</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b8e8cd0-4702-4d6d-97cb-d1b8d236e815"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete an actual project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02939: (2026x) Deleting and executing Macros

- page_id: `272735567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735567/2026x+Deleting+and+executing+Macros
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:32.599+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851813206

851813217

851813204

**On this page:**

851813216

You can click the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button in the **Organize Macros** dialog to delete or execute a selected macro. You can also press the pre-designed mnemonic keys to delete or run a macro as described in****Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys below.

##### Deleting a Macro

To delete a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros to open the Organize Macros dialog.
2. Select a macro from the table and either click Delete or press Alt + D . A dialog will open, asking whether you want to delete the macro, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click **Yes** and the macro will be deleted from the **Organize Macros** dialog.

##### Executing a Macro

To execute a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='organize-macros.png']
2. Select a macro from the table and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . After the macro has been executed, a message will open: The macro <macro name> has been executed .
3. If you have the parameters in the **Macro Information** dialog, you need to specify the value in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, see figure below, before running the macro. For an array datatype, you need to click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the **Value** column in the **Macro Arguments** dialog and enter each value into each line. The value of the first line will be the value in array index 0.An ElementPath is a Qualified Name. You can find this information in the specification dialog of each element, see figure below.The **Macro Arguments** dialog, see the figure below, will be displayed if the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is not selected.If you want to save argument values in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, you need to select the **Set as default values** check box before you click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can also execute a macro from the main browser by pressing the shortcut keys that you have defined in the Organize Macros dialog.
- You can only run macro one at a time.
- If there is an error while running a macro, for example, syntax error, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot execute the <macro language> macro, please make sure that <path, filename, extension> is correct. <error description> .
- If the modeling tool cannot find a macro file in the location that you have specified in the Open dialog, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot find the macro: <path, filename, extension> .

##### Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys

Macro Engine also provides mnemonic keys to add, edit, delete, and run a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog. The table below shows the **Organize Macros** dialog mnemonic keys and their function.

*Table listing of Organize Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys*

| Mnemonic keys | Button | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Alt + A |  | To add a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + E |  | To edit a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + D |  | To delete a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + O |  | Code To edit source code in Macro Editor. |
| Alt + R |  | To run a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + C |  | To click the Close button. |
| Alt + H |  | To click the Help button. |

- You can click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Organize Macros dialog, see figure above, to edit and save source code in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.
- You can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the source code or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to run the macro in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba6abb75-42b8-4f40-b744-30b4aebb4d06"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851813206</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91306ecd-be05-4444-af78-d85e7ccae0f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851813217</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="699757f1-8046-490b-8727-712e93a1ec1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851813204</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d22fc56-6ce2-4584-b32f-3fe6de78e1f8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="317d9e0c-4ad3-455e-9c9d-3c7e66e9a5e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851813216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click the <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deleting and executing Macros" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> or <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deleting and executing Macros" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> button in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog to delete or execute a selected macro. You can find this information in the specification dialog of each element, see figure below.</li><li>The <strong>Macro Arguments</strong> dialog, see the figure below, will be displayed if the<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deleting and executing Macros" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> check box is not selected.</li><li>If you want to save argument values in the <strong>Macro Arguments</strong> dialog, you need to select the <strong>Set as default values</strong> check box before you click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Specification of class" ac:alt="Specification of class"><ri:attachment ri:filename="spec-of-class.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deleting and executing Macros" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Macro Arguments Dialog" ac:alt="Macro Arguments Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fig 17.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deleting and executing Macros" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62b27853-7ba5-4395-9c7d-9b9929311c76"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can also execute a macro from the main browser by pressing the shortcut keys that you have defined in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog.</li><li>You can only run macro one at a time.</li><li>If there is an error while running a macro, for example, syntax error, the following message will open: <strong>&lt;Modeling tool name&gt; ## PAGE 02940: (2026x) Deleting Milestones from Lr diagram
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete a Project from the chart

1. Select a Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Project from the model

1. Select a Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

## PAGE 02943: (2026x) Dependency

- page_id: `272738158`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738158/2026x+Dependency
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:40.928+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) Process Definition Diagram > (2026x) BPMN Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1488402433

1488402435

1488402435

**Description**

A Dependency indicates a semantic relationship between two model elements (or two sets of model elements). Dependency indicates a situation in which a change to a supplier (target) element may require a change to a client (source) element in the dependency.

A Dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between model elements. The model element at the start point of the arrow (the client element) depends on the model element at the arrowhead (the supplier element). The arrow can be labeled with an optional individual name.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1488402432

**Related element**

- BPMN Process

**Related diagram**

- Process Definition Diagram

Dependency indicates a situation in which a change to a supplier (target) element may require a change to a client (source) element in the dependency.</p><p>A Dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between model elements. The model element at the start point of the arrow (the client element) depends on the model element at the arrowhead (the supplier element). The arrow can be labeled with an optional individual name.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="059774f3-a82c-447e-a007-6dbd72ad9663"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488402432</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Process+Definition+Diagram">Process Definition Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732629 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02944: (2026x) Dependency Matrix

- page_id: `272732629`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732629/2026x+Dependency+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:01.496+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Analysis diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Demo introducing Dependency Matix.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The Dependency Matrix is a quick method for representing dependencies compactly. Diagrams, UML elements, and extended UML elements serve as row and column entries. The cells in the matrix show where these elements are associated (related).

Dependency matrices can show dependency criteria, such as

- UML relationships
- BPML, SysML v1, UPDM, and other DSL relationships
- Semantic dependencies (dependencies through properties)
- Relationships through tags (tags allow for relating UML elements that cannot be represented on the same diagram)
- Transitive relationships (through the OCL or meta chain)

Dependency matrices are useful for

- Quickly visualizing and creating dependency criteria.
- Compactly visualizing relations of a large system whose system relations cannot be represented by a diagram on a single sheet of paper due to the size of the system.
- Studying relations from a particular scope and type of element by filtering the unimportant data. Hierarchical columns allow for browsing directly in the created matrix through the model scope and visualize the required area.
- Showing relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, such as a representation (a class by the lifeline), a behavior representation in other diagrams, an operation representation by Call Behavior Action, Use Case relations with describing activities through the property Owned Behavior, etc. The semantic dependency matrix is needed for deeper model analysis. The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.

To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new dependency matrix type' space='UPDG'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01f32448-f2c6-483d-b046-12f186af6ed0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Demo introducing Dependency Matix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4401ba9-3355-484d-a927-d0252bd8340b"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7Utc3D2vU1I" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01079131-6990-4c53-b5b2-3ee1cdc343d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Dependency Matrix is a quick method for representing dependencies compactly. Diagrams, UML elements, and extended UML elements serve as row and column entries. The cells in the matrix show where these elements are associated (related).</p><p>Dependency matrices can show dependency criteria, such as</p><ul><li data-uuid="540837f3-6e04-4f6d-8a77-878b14704f01">UML relationships</li><li data-uuid="ac4e49ee-e816-414c-8265-2c26adfad653">BPML, SysML v1, UPDM, and other DSL relationships</li><li data-uuid="2ca43f87-f1e0-4531-b740-f6d29af03d6f">Semantic dependencies (dependencies through properties)</li><li data-uuid="db395e70-4a15-438c-b01d-3b49cd5f016d">Relationships through tags (tags allow for relating UML elements that cannot be represented on the same diagram)</li><li data-uuid="58d8ed6a-937d-4bdf-9071-e1c91ae16712">Transitive relationships (through the OCL or meta chain)</li></ul><p>Dependency matrices are useful for</p><ul><li data-uuid="1a40e542-7051-4e59-be2c-39ec95089727">Quickly visualizing and creating dependency criteria.</li><li data-uuid="9c0d5a4f-1b96-49e9-add3-085df6b0d406">Compactly visualizing relations of a large system whose system relations cannot be represented by a diagram on a single sheet of paper due to the size of the system.</li><li data-uuid="a5ceb5fe-367e-41c0-8a4a-1432bdb9a48b">Studying relations from a particular scope and type of element by filtering the unimportant data. Hierarchical columns allow for browsing directly in the created matrix through the model scope and visualize the required area.</li><li data-uuid="f38ef2ec-9400-4cf0-a7e3-2229cab368ef"><p>Showing relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, such as a representation (a class by the lifeline), a behavior representation in other diagrams, an operation representation by Call Behavior Action, Use Case relations with describing activities through the property Owned Behavior, etc. The semantic dependency matrix is needed for deeper model analysis. The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="525fa5a5-ef0d-406e-9c44-f625e82fc12d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Creating new dependency matrix type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736830 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02945: (2026x) Dependency Matrix Criteria area

- page_id: `272736830`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736830/2026x+Dependency+Matrix+Criteria+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:49.734+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Analysis diagrams > (2026x) Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes (except for the Dependency Criteria) in the **Criteria** area.

See the descriptions of the dependency matrix Criteria area in the following table.

| Element Name | Element Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Row Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element type or multiple element types to show in rows of the dependency matrix. |
| Column Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element or multiple element types to show in columns of the dependency matrix. |
| Row Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in rows of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define row scope. |
| Column Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in columns of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define column scope. |
| Dependency Criteria | Text box with | Click the ... button to define what relationships between row and column elements you wish to display in the dependency matrix cells. |
| Direction | Drop-down list | Select a direction of relationships for the relationship analysis and representation in the dependency matrix. Be aware that new relationships will be created with the selected direction. |
| Show Elements | Drop-down list | Select to show only related (by a selected dependency criteria), only non-related, or all elements. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736837 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02947: (2026x) Dependency Matrix shortcut menu

- page_id: `272736837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736837/2026x+Dependency+Matrix+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:51.251+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Analysis diagrams > (2026x) Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the dependency matrix shortcut menu

- Right-click an empty space on one of the following [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_shortcut_menu.png']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency Matrix Criteria area' space='']
  - Matrix pane

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specification (Enter) | Opens the of a dependency matrix. |
| Go To | Navigates back or forward to the diagram that was open in the current diagram tab, finds and navigates to diagrams wherein the matrix is used, or to the matrix owner in the , or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship. |
| Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Selects the dependency matrix in the of the . |
| Related Elements | Allows selecting whether to create a for the matrix or search for model elements which depends on the matrix or are used by the matrix. |
| Refactor | the matrix to a diagram indicated by the user. |
| Tools | Opens a list of that are available for the matrix. |
| Print Active Diagram | Prints a matrix. |
| Show Tabs in Full Screen (F11) | Shows a matrix in the full screen mode. Click Close Full Screen to return to the previous view. |
| Close Tab (Ctrl+F4) | Closes an active matrix. |
| Close All Tabs But Current (Ctrl+Shift+F4) | Closes all diagrams except an active one. |
| Close All Tabs(Ctrl+Alt+F4) | Closes all opened diagrams. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735382 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02948: (2026x) Dependency Matrix tool

- page_id: `272735382`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735382/2026x+Dependency+Matrix+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:19.704+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352667888

1352667888

1352667888

The Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to:

- Access data from Dependency Matrix.
- Use diagrams to get data from Dependency Matrix.
- Get row nodes and elements.
- Get column nodes and elements.
- Get dependencies in a cell (between specific row and column nodes).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Dependency Matrix diagram.

Like other Custom tools, the Generic Table tool '*dependencymatrixtool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tool' space='']*.

To import the Dependency Matrix tool to a template, type the following code in the template:

```text

```

1352667887

**Related pages**

For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see section <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom tool" /></ac:link></em>.</p><p>To import the Dependency Matrix tool to a template, type the following code in the template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60b1715a-4f98-4144-8e2f-fe560aeabf6a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("depmatrix","com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1206f0ed-5098-4881-8bdb-c970db001d9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352667887</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="58a9d83e-5455-4a23-8fa0-22a48a2c3fda" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738941 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02949: (2026x) Dependency matrix tool API

- page_id: `272738941`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738941/2026x+Dependency+matrix+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:50.036+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Dependency Matrix tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352819143

1352819146

1352819140

**On this page**

4

1352819146

Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to access data from a Dependency Matrix, use diagrams to get data, row elements, column names, or relations between row and column elements from the matrix.

You can use the following Dependency Matrix tool API in the template.

**Class DependencyMatrixTool**

Use the following methods to use diagrams to get data.

- +getMatrix( diagram : Diagram) : Matrix
- +getMatrix( diagramName : String) : Matrix

**Class Matrix**

Use the following methods to get matrix row elements or nodes, column elements or nodes, and dependencies between these nodes/elements.

- +getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getCellDependencies( rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)
- +getRowElements() : List<Element>
- +getColumnElements() : List<Element>
- +getRelation( row: Element, column : Element) : List<Relation>

**Class Relation**

Describes dependency in a Dependency Matrix cell. Use the following methods for class relations.

- +getName() : String
- +getElement() : Element
- +getSemanticType() : String
- +getDirection() : String

##### Using Diagrams to Get Data from Dependency Matrix

A Dependency Matrix is a special diagram. You can retrieve it using the $Diagram variable. Thus, every method must accept a diagram instance or a diagram’s name. Use the following methods to return a Dependency Matrix instance. You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements

**getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram element

Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.

For example:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Names

**getMatrix(diagramName : String) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram’s name.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram’s name

Return a Dependency Matrix instance.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting Rows and Columns

The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.

##### Getting All Rows

**getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.

To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting all Columns

**getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.

To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns

**getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)**

Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNode – ElementNode of a row
- columnNode – ElementNode of a column

The returned value is a list of Relations.

The Relation class contains the following methods:

- getSemanticType : String Return the semantic type
- getElement : Element Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.
- getName : String Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name; otherwise, return a human name.
- getDirection : String Return the direction.

To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:

| #import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,["Dependency Matrix"]))#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))$bookmark.create($diagram.name) |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol |  |
| #forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))->#end#endcol | #endrow |
| #endpage |  |  |

or nodes, column elements or nodes, and dependencies between these nodes/elements.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRowNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRowElements() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getColumnElements() : List&lt;Element&gt; </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRelation( row: Element, Use the following methods for class relations.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getName() : String</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getElement() : Element </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getSemanticType() : String </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getDirection() : String</li></ul><h3>Using Diagrams to Get Data from Dependency Matrix</h3><p>A Dependency Matrix is a special diagram. You can retrieve it using the $Diagram variable. Thus, every method must accept a diagram instance or a diagram’s name. Use the following methods to return a Dependency Matrix instance. You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.</p><h3>Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements</h3><p><strong>getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix</strong><br />To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fb3ec99-7085-4d08-a2ce-b6ee4e90c5aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – a diagram element</li></ul><p>Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1eeac84-576f-424b-a258-933e831a39f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
````html
#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram)) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Names</h3><p><strong>getMatrix(diagramName : String) : Matrix</strong> <br />To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram’s name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="076989fa-ed89-4186-9c35-315bb3c52b5f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>diagram – a diagram’s name</li></ul><p>Return a Dependency Matrix instance.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cb801ad-449e-4a77-8e55-9262a5d0f37b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($diagram = "diagram name")
#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Rows and Columns</h3><p>The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.</p><h3>Getting All Rows</h3><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">getRowNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</span></strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a742c90-fbaa-40f9-8fc1-fc0ab63d716a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.</p><p>To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65eabb1b-3fbe-49bd-bbc9-a58dd91223f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($rowNode in $matrix.rowNodes) 
$rowNode.element.name 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all Columns</h3><p><strong>getColumnNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="778b1227-f91a-4dca-929f-7e764a388888"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.</p><p>To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b930c55b-1af1-4d2d-a759-56410c12be65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($col in $matrix.columns) 
$colNode.element.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns</h3><p><strong>getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3cfac07-2d4a-4984-9227-d082e318c001"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getCellDependencies($rowNode, $columnNode)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>rowNode –  ElementNode of a row</li><li>columnNode –  ElementNode of a column</li></ul><p>The returned value is a list of Relations.</p><p>The Relation class contains the following methods:</p><ul><li><strong>getSemanticType</strong> : String<br />             Return the semantic type</li><li><strong>getElement</strong> : Element <br />             Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.</li><li><strong>getName</strong> : String <br />             Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name;              otherwise, return a human name.</li><li><strong>getDirection</strong> : String <br />             Return the direction.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722e0feb-cfdd-4eea-b81e-ddf75989285c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $matrix.rowNodes) 
   #foreach($col in $matrix.columnNodes)
      #foreach($rel in $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col))
         $row.element.name has $rel.name with $col.element.name
      #end
   #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 90.779%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 53.1113%;" /><col style="width: 40.3155%;" /><col style="width: 6.57318%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><pre>#import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')</pre><pre>#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,[&quot;Dependency Matrix&quot;]))</pre><pre>#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))</pre><pre>$bookmark.create($diagram.name)</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre>#forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre><br /></pre><pre><br /></pre><pre>#forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name</pre></td><td><pre>#forcol ($col in </pre><pre>$matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))</pre><pre>-&gt;#end#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><pre>#endrow</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>#endpage</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736425 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02950: (2026x) Dependency Matrix toolbar

- page_id: `272736425`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736425/2026x+Dependency+Matrix+toolbar
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:18.066+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Analysis diagrams > (2026x) Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the detailed descriptions of the dependency matrix toolbar buttons in the following table.

| Button | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Back (Alt+Left) | Navigates back to the previous diagram that was open in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Forward (Atl+Right) | Navigates forward to the diagram in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Opens the in the and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree. |
|  | Delete (Ctrl+D) | Deletes the selected element from the matrix and model.If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again. |
|  | Remove From Matrix (Delete) | Removes the selected element from the matrix. The selected element is not removed from the model. |
|  | Change Axes | Transposes rows and columns. |
|  | Export | Exports a matrix as a Comma Separated Values ( .csv) file. The file can be opened with Microsoft Excel. |
|  | Refresh | Refreshes the matrix after updating the criteria or data. |
|  | Validate Diagram | Validates the matrix against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored. |
|  | Options | Expands the menu with other options. |
|  | Specification - opens the matrix Specification window wherein you can specify matrix properties.Legend Location - lists legend location options and selection to not show it.Include Additional Content - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified row/column scope. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the dependency matrix, its content is collected and represented in the matrix. |  |
|  | Suppress/Expand Criteria Area | Suppresses or expands the matrix Criteria area. |

/></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Remove From <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Matrix (Delete)</span></td><td><p>Removes the selected element from the matrix. The selected element is not removed from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change_axes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency Matrix toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Change Axes</td><td>Transposes rows and columns.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_as_cvs.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency Matrix toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Export</td><td>Exports a matrix as a Comma Separated Values ( .csv) file. The <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">file can be opened with Microsoft Excel.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency Matrix toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Refresh</td><td>Refreshes the matrix after updating the criteria or data.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Validate Diagram</td><td colspan="1">Validates the matrix against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Options</td><td><p>Expands the menu with other options.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><br /></td><td colspan="2"><ul><li><strong>Specification</strong> - opens the matrix Specification window wherein you can specify matrix properties.</li><li><strong>Legend Location</strong> - l<span>ists legend location options and selection to not show it.</span></li><li><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified row/column scope. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the dependency matrix, its content is collected and represented in the matrix.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Suppress_expand_crit_area.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Suppress/Expand Criteria Area</p></td><td colspan="1">Suppresses or expands the matrix <strong>Criteria</strong> area.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733608 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02952: (2026x) Deployment diagram

- page_id: `272733608`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733608/2026x+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:36.913+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) UML diagrams
- labels: ['implementation', 'implementation-diagram', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308620457

308620459

308620458

**Overview**

A Deployment diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It focuses on showing the physical layout of various hardware components ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MED']), e.g. CPU, a database server, and an application server that compose a system as well as the distribution of executable programs (software components) on hardware. For instance, to describe a website, the Deployment diagram would show what hardware components exist, what software components run, and how the different pieces are interconnected. Deployment diagrams are crucial when dealing with distributed systems.

**Purpose**

Using a Deployment diagram, you can show the actual computers and devices (Nodes), along with the connections they have to each other, thus specifying a system topology. Inside the Nodes, executable [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MED'] and objects are located in a way that it shows where the software units are residing and on which Nodes they are executed. You may also show dependencies between Components.

**Usage**

A Deployment diagram can be used to:

- show the structure of the run-time system
- capture the hardware that is going to be used to implement the system and links between different hardware items
- model physical hardware elements and the communication paths between them
- plan the system architecture
- document the deployment of software components or nodes.

**Summary**

Deployment diagrams are valuable because they can be:

- used to model the hardware platform for a system
- used to identify hardware capabilities that have an impact on the performance planning and software configuration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Deployment diagram

308620456

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']

It focuses on showing the physical layout of various hardware components (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Node" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), e.g. CPU, a database server, and an application server that compose a system as well as the distribution of executable programs (software components) on hardware. For instance, to describe a website, the Deployment diagram would show what hardware components exist, what software components run, and how the different pieces are interconnected. Deployment diagrams are crucial when dealing with distributed systems.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>Using a Deployment diagram, you can show the actual computers and devices (Nodes), along with the connections they have to each other, thus specifying a system topology. Inside the Nodes, executable <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Component" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and objects are located in a way that it shows where the software units are residing and on which Nodes they are executed. You may also show dependencies between Components.</p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Deployment diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>show the structure of the run-time system</li><li>capture the hardware that is going to be used to implement the system and links between different hardware items</li><li>model physical hardware elements and the communication paths between them</li><li>plan the system architecture</li><li>document the deployment of software components or nodes. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Deployment diagrams are valuable because they can be:</p><ul><li>used to model the hardware platform for a system</li><li>used to identify hardware capabilities that have an impact on the performance planning and software configuration. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Deployment diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="292ab185-bc83-4d52-bf5f-8dc8aad28c4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308620456</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729973 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02953: (2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn

- page_id: `272729973`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729973/2026x+Deprecated+JavaScript+Nashorn
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:20.984+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine > (2026x) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

965417895

INLINE

965417916

INLINE

965417905

INLINE

The JavaScript Nashorn language has been removed from the modeling tool. You can learn more about deprecated JavaScript Nashorn [here](https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/335). Please use JavaScript Rhino instead. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='JavaScript migration from Nashorn to Rhino engine' space=''] page.

All JavaScript Nashorn usages need to be changed to JavaScript Rhino in projects to work correctly. Below are the recommended steps for achieving that:

1. Use one of the following options to run the modeling tool:
  - Proceeding with this option means that none of your project's JavaScript Nashorn scripts will work until they are changed to or rewritten in JavaScript Rhino.
  - Proceeding with this option means that your project's JavaScript Nashorn scripts will be executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine; thus, the majority of the scripts will work, but they will still need to be changed to or rewritten in JavaScript Rhino.
2. 
3. 

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram showing the JavaScript Nashorn usages' change to JavaScript Rhino.

##### Running the modeling tool

###### Option 1. Run modeling tool without executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts (default)

Following this option means:

- The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are not executed, meaning that the model will not function as expected due to script failures.
- This is the default option; simply run the modeling tool and open a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed.

###### Option 2. Run modeling tool executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts with JavaScript Rhino engine

Following this option means:

- The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine. Due to the similarities between Rhino and Nashorn, most of the project's scripts are likely to work.
- To proceed with this option, you need to add an additional system property, as described below.

To enable JavaScript Nashorn script execution with JavaScript Rhino engine

1. Open the <modeling tool installation directory>\bin\<modeling tool name>.properties file.
2. Locate the JAVA_ARGS= line
3. Add the following system property: -Dexecute.legacy.javascript.as.rhino=true
4. Restart the modeling tool.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, the 'JavaScript Nashorn (deprecated)' error notification is displayed.

##### Opening a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages

Opening a project that contains JavaScript *Nashorn* scripts immediately displays a notification about the removal of the JavaScript *Nashorn* scripting language. The notification is displayed only once upon the first opening of the project with the new version of the tool. You can click the **Find Usages**link to run the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation suite to locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn (for more information, see the section below)*.*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Finding and resolving JavaScript Nashorn**usages**

The recommended steps to find and resolve JavaScript Nashorn usages:

1. Run the Deprecated JavaScript validation suite to .
2. Evaluate each script failing the validation and rewrite it in JavaScript Rhino if necessary. Due to the similarities between JavaScript Rhino and JavaScript Nashorn, most of the project's Nashorn scripts are likely to work with the Rhino engine. However, a review is recommended as to whether the Nashorn scripts' execution with the Rhino engine are accurate. If not, the Nashorn script must be modified to work in Rhino.
3. via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver.

###### Finding JavaScript Nashorn**usages

To locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn in a project, we recommend running the**Deprecated JavaScript**validation suite (**Analyze**>**Validation**>**Validate**). The validation suite contains the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation rule, which:

- detects all JavaScript Nashorn**usages;
- allows you to change the JavaScript Nashorn**language to JavaScript Rhino**for implemented expressions via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### After running the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation suite, hold Ctrl+A to select all detected instances of JavaScript *Nashorn* and resolve them via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino** solver in the Validation Results panel.

For a detailed description of the validation rule, see the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript' space='']** page.

###### Resolving JavaScript Nashorn**usages

The **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver changes the used scripting language without affecting the script itself. In many cases, changing the used scripting language will not affect the functionality of the script. Otherwise, the 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification (shown below) will appear, requiring you to rewrite the script in JavaScript Rhino.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Run modeling tool without executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts (default)</h4><p>Following this option means:</p><ul><li>The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are not executed, meaning that the model will not function as expected due to script failures.</li><li>This is the default option; simply run the modeling tool and open a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expression_evaluation_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed.</h6><h4>Option 2. Run modeling tool executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts with JavaScript Rhino engine</h4><p>Following this option means:</p><ul><li>The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine. Due to the similarities between Rhino and Nashorn, most of the project's scripts are likely to work.</li><li>To proceed with this option, you need to add an additional system property, as described below.</li></ul><p>To enable JavaScript Nashorn script execution with JavaScript Rhino engine</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin\&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file.</li><li>Locate the <em>JAVA_ARGS=</em> line</li><li>Add the following system property:<br /><em>-Dexecute.legacy.javascript.as.rhino=true</em></li><li><span>Restart the modeling tool.</span></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deprecated_Nashorn_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, the 'JavaScript Nashorn (deprecated)' error notification is displayed.</h6><h3>Opening a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages</h3><p>Opening a project that contains JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> scripts immediately displays a notification about the removal of the JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> scripting language. The notification is displayed only once upon the first opening of the project with the new version of the tool. You can click the <strong>Find Usages </strong>link to run the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong> validation suite to locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn (for more information, see the <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Finding and resolving JavaScript Nashorn usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section below)<em>.</em></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Discontinued_Nashorn_notification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><strong>Finding and resolving <span>JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>usages</span></strong></h3><p>The recommended steps to find and resolve JavaScript Nashorn usages:</p><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Run the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong><span> validation suite to <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[find all JavaScript Nashorn usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p>Evaluate each script failing the validation and rewrite it in JavaScript Rhino if necessary.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00e77b0b-72b5-4a81-87c2-56b74adc6f65"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the similarities between JavaScript Rhino and JavaScript Nashorn, most of the project's Nashorn scripts are likely to work with the Rhino engine.
