<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732439 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02954: (2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model

- page_id: `272732439`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732439/2026x+Description+of+State+Machines+used+in+this+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:35.879+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Humidifier system sample
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this model, there are two main components that interacted with each other: the room and the humidifier system. The purpose of the humidifier is to control the humidity of the room once a user has selected a humidity level for the room. State machines are used to show the control of humidity in the room, both to depict the user’s input and the humidifier’s control system. For the sake of brevity, the following SysML v1 diagrams form only the core set of diagrams from the SysML v1 model, and include all three state machines used in this example.

1. HumidifierSystem Internal Block Diagram : This diagram is part of the HumidifierSystem block. The flow between the part properties room (typed by HumidifiedRoom ) and humidifier (typed by Humidifier ) are shown to be going through ports. These ports are the interfaces between the two system components, and contain numeric information about the humidity and vapor going between the two components. [ATTACHMENT filename='humidifier_system_ibd.png']
2. Humidifier Internal Block Diagram : This diagram is part of the Humidifier block. The flow within the humidifier system are depicted in this diagram. With information about the room’s current humidity from the *humidity_in* port, and control signal from the humidity control system from the part property control (typed by*Control*), the part property *heaterControl*(typed by *HeaterControl*) uses a state machine to decide whether or not to turn on the heater control system in order to generate vapor in the part property *vaporgenerationplant*(typed by *VaporGenerationPlant*). The part property*vaporgenerationplant* also uses information from the part property control to determine the amount of vapor released into the room (and consumed from the part property*watertank*, typed by*WaterTank*) as well as let *control* know the temperature of water in the vapor generation system. The part property *control* also uses information from when the user wants to turn on the humidifier from the part property *usage* (typed by *UsageScenario*) and information about the amount of water in the water tank from the part property*watertank*. [ATTACHMENT filename='humidifier_ibd.png']
3. UsageScenarioStateMachine State Machine Diagram : This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property usage in the Humidifier internal block diagram and it determines when (as in, at which times) the humidifier system should humidify the room. [ATTACHMENT filename='usage_scenario_stm.png']
4. ControlStateMachine State Machine Diagram : This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property control in the Humidifier internal block diagram and it determines the operation of the heater control system heatercontrol and the vapor generation plant vaporgenerationplant based on information about the water levels in the tank from the watertank , the temperature of vaporization from the vaporgenerationplant , and the operation of when the humidifier system should be started from usage . [ATTACHMENT filename='control_stm.png']
5. HeaterControlSM State Machine Diagram : This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property heaterControl in the Humidifier internal block diagram. It uses information from the room’s current humidity from the humidity_in pin, the target humidity from the part property targetHumidity , and the control signal from control in order to decide whether or not to turn on the heater control system for the generation of vapor with vaporgenerationplant . [ATTACHMENT filename='heater_control_stm.png']
6. VaporGenerationPlant Internal Block Diagram : This internal block diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flow associated with the part property vaporgenerationplant in the Humidifier internal block diagram. Given the control signal from control for its water fan and the control signal from the heaterControl , after the numeric calculations it gives information about the water temperature to the control as well as information about the generated vapor from the watertank and for the room. [ATTACHMENT filename='vaporGenerationPlantCalculation_ibd.png']
7. WaterTank Parametric Diagram : This parametric diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property watertank in the Humidifier internal block diagram. Given the information about water consumed for vapor generation from vaporgenerationplant , numeric calculations provide information about the volume of remaining water and this new information is sent to the control part property. [ATTACHMENT filename='water_tank_par.png']
8. **HumidifiedRoom Internal Block Diagram**: This diagram is part of the *HumidifiedRoom* block. This internal block diagram depicts the information flow of the vapor going into the room and the humidity that results from this vaporization. The information about the vapor being inputted into the room goes through a unit conversion with the part property *mLpH2mLpS* (typed by block *VolumeConversion*) before passing on the information to part property*rH* (typed by *RelativeHumidity*). This information, along with information about current relative humidity of the room from part property*rH* and information about the saturated vapor pressure from part property*sVP* (typed by *SaturationVaporPressure*), is used to calculate the amount of humidity in the room. The part property*toPercentage* (typed by *PercentageConversion*) converts the signal about room humidity from fractions into a percentage value and sends this new information about the percentage value back to the humidifier system. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. SaturationVaporPressure Parametric Diagram : This parametric diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property sVP (typed by SaturationVaporPressure ) in the HumidifiedRoom internal block diagram. Numerical calculations in this diagram provide information about the room’s vapor pressure to be sent as a signal used to calculate current room humidity in the HumidifiedRoom internal block diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='saturation_vapor_pressure_par.png']
10. RelativeHumidity Internal Block Diagram : This internal block diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property rH (typed by RelativeHumidity ) in the HumidifiedRoom internal block diagram. It calculates the current humidity of the room given the room’s vapor pressure levels as well as the incoming vapor coming from the humidifier system. [ATTACHMENT filename='relative_humidity_ibd.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this model, there are two main components that interacted with each other: the room and the humidifier system. The purpose of the humidifier is to control the humidity of the room once a user has selected a humidity level for the room. State machines are used to show the control of humidity in the room, both to depict the user’s input and the humidifier’s control system. For the sake of brevity, the following SysML v1 diagrams form only the core set of diagrams from the SysML v1 model, and include all three state machines used in this example.</p><ol><li><strong>HumidifierSystem Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This diagram is part of the <em>HumidifierSystem </em>block. The flow between the part properties <em>room</em> (typed by <em>HumidifiedRoom</em>) and <em>humidifier</em> (typed by <em>Humidifier</em>) are shown to be going through ports. These ports are the interfaces between the two system components, and contain numeric information about the humidity and vapor going between the two components.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="humidifier_system_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><strong>Humidifier Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This diagram is part of the <em>Humidifier</em> block. The flow within the humidifier system are depicted in this diagram. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">With information about the room’s current humidity from the <em>humidity_in</em> port, and control signal from the humidity control system from the part property control (typed by<em> Control</em>), the part property <em>heaterControl </em>(typed by <em>HeaterControl</em>) uses a state machine to decide whether or not to turn on the heater control system in order to generate vapor in the part property <em>vaporgenerationplant </em>(typed by <em>VaporGenerationPlant</em>). The part property<em> vaporgenerationplant</em> also uses information from the part property control to determine the amount of vapor released into the room (and consumed from the part property<em> watertank</em>, typed by<em> WaterTank</em>) as well as let <em>control</em>  know the temperature of water in the vapor generation system. The part property <em>control</em> also uses information from when the user wants to turn on the humidifier from the part property <em>usage</em> (typed by <em>UsageScenario</em>) and information about the amount of water in the water tank from the part property<em> watertank</em>.</span><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="humidifier_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>UsageScenarioStateMachine State Machine Diagram</strong>: This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property usage in the <em>Humidifier </em>internal block diagram and it determines when (as in, at which times) the humidifier system should humidify the room.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usage_scenario_stm.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>ControlStateMachine State Machine Diagram</strong>: This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property<em> control  </em>in the <em>Humidifier </em>internal block diagram and it determines the operation of the heater control system <em>heatercontrol  </em>and the vapor generation plant <em>vaporgenerationplant</em> based on information about the water levels in the tank from the <em>watertank</em>, the temperature of vaporization from the <em>vaporgenerationplant</em>, and the operation of when the humidifier system should be started from<em> usage</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="control_stm.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>HeaterControlSM State Machine Diagram</strong>: This state machine diagram depicts the behavior of the part property<em> heaterControl</em> in the <em>Humidifier </em>internal block diagram. It uses information from the room’s current humidity from the <em>humidity_in  </em>pin, the target humidity from the part property<em> targetHumidity</em>, and the control signal from<em> control  </em>in order to decide whether or not to turn on the heater control system for the generation of vapor with <em>vaporgenerationplant</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="197"><ri:attachment ri:filename="heater_control_stm.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>VaporGenerationPlant Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This internal block diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flow associated with the part property <em>vaporgenerationplant</em> in the <em>Humidifier </em>internal block diagram. Given the control signal from <em>control  </em>for its water fan and the control signal from the <em>heaterControl</em>, after the numeric calculations it gives information about the water temperature to the <em>control</em> as well as information about the generated vapor from the<em> watertank</em> and for the room.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="vaporGenerationPlantCalculation_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>WaterTank Parametric Diagram</strong>: This parametric diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property <em>watertank</em> in the <em>Humidifier </em>internal block diagram. Given the information about water consumed for vapor generation from <em>vaporgenerationplant</em>, numeric calculations provide information about the volume of remaining water and this new information is sent to the<em> control</em> part property.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="water_tank_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p><strong>HumidifiedRoom Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This diagram is part of the <em>HumidifiedRoom</em> block. This internal block diagram depicts the information flow of the vapor going into the room and the humidity that results from this vaporization. The information about the vapor being inputted into the room goes through a unit conversion with the part property <em>mLpH2mLpS</em> (typed by block <em>VolumeConversion</em>) before passing on the information to part property<em> rH</em> (typed by <em>RelativeHumidity</em>). This information, along with information about current relative humidity of the room from part property<em> rH</em> and information about the saturated vapor pressure from part property<em> sVP</em> (typed by <em>SaturationVaporPressure</em>), is used to calculate the amount of humidity in the room. The part property<em> toPercentage</em> (typed by <em>PercentageConversion</em>) converts the signal about room humidity from fractions into a percentage value and sends this new information about the percentage value back to the humidifier system.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="humidified_room_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><strong>SaturationVaporPressure Parametric Diagram</strong>: This parametric diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property <em>sVP</em> (typed by<em> SaturationVaporPressure</em>) in the<em> HumidifiedRoom</em> internal block diagram. Numerical calculations in this diagram provide information about the room’s vapor pressure to be sent as a signal used to calculate current room humidity in the <em>HumidifiedRoom </em>internal block diagram.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="saturation_vapor_pressure_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>RelativeHumidity Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This internal block diagram depicts numerical calculations for the informational flows associated with the part property <em>rH</em> (typed by <em>RelativeHumidity</em>) in the <em>HumidifiedRoom </em>internal block diagram. It calculates the current humidity of the room given the room’s vapor pressure levels as well as the incoming vapor coming from the humidifier system.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relative_humidity_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of State Machines used in this model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732268 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02955: (2026x) Description of the ElectricCircuit.mdzip SysML v1 models

- page_id: `272732268`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732268/2026x+Description+of+the+ElectricCircuit.mdzip+SysML+v1+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:24.059+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Electric Circuit sample
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this model an electric circuit is built using a source, a ground, two resistors, a capacitor, an inductor, as well as ports that allow to connect these circuit components to show the flow (and potential to flow) of charge from one component to the other. In total, there are 8 SysML v1 diagrams in this model:

1. CircuitComponents Block Definition Diagram : This diagram contains a block describing a generic two-pin (ports) electrical component class, TwoPinElectricalComponent , that serves as a generalization of other blocks. The generalization contains a block describing the Resistor class and its properties, a block describing the Inductor class and its properties, a block describing the Capacitor class and its properties, and a block describing the Source class and its properties. There is also a block describing the Ground class that is needed as a reference point for the flow potential (voltage) of charged particles in an electric circuit. [ATTACHMENT filename='circuit_components_bdd.png']
2. CircuitConstraints Block Definition Diagram : Mathematical expressions are provided in constraint blocks ResistorConstraint , CapacitorConstraint , InductorConstraint , and SourceConstraint that extend the mathematical constraints and parameters defined in BinaryElectricalComponentConstraint for each of their respective constraint block classes. These four constraint blocks, along with the mathematical expressions in the GroundConstraint constraint block, describe the flow and potential to flow behavior of charged electric particles that go through different components in the electric circuit. [ATTACHMENT filename='circuit_constraints_bdd.png']
3. Circuit Internal Block Diagram : This diagram is part of the Circuit block, and it shows layout of components in the electric circuit design. The links between the ports of the components depict the physical interactions that occur in the electric domain, that is the flow (current) and potential to flow (voltage) of the charged particles that move through the electric circuit. [ATTACHMENT filename='electric_circuit_ibd.png']
4. Capacitor Parametric Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the CapacitorConstraint constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the Capacitor block (from the CircuitComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='capacitor_par.png']
5. Ground **Parametric**Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the GroundConstraint constraint block are bound to the potential to flow property of the Ground block’s port (from the CircuitComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='ground_par.png']
6. Inductor **Parametric**Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the InductorConstraint constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the Inductor block (from the CircuitComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='inductor_par.png']
7. Resistor **Parametric**Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the ResistorConstraint constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the Resistor block (from the CircuitComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='resistor_par.png']
8. **Source **Parametric**Diagram**: The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the *SourceConstraint*constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *Source*block (from the *CircuitComponents* block definition diagram). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732289 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02956: (2026x) Description of the Hudraulics.mdzip SysML v1 models

- page_id: `272732289`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732289/2026x+Description+of+the+Hudraulics.mdzip+SysML+v1+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:24.900+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Hydraulics sample
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this model two tanks filled with unequal amounts of a fluid are connected at the bottom of each tank by a pipe. The flow of the fluid from the tank with more fluid to the tank with less fluid is modeled in SysML v1, where it can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape for simulation. There are 5 SysML diagrams in this model:

1. **TwoTanksComponents Block Definition Diagram**: This diagram contains a block describing the *Tank* class and its properties, a block describing the *Pipe*class and its properties, and interface block describing the conserved substance flow rate and potential to flow. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. TwoTankConstraints Block Definition Diagram : Mathematical expressions are provided in constraint blocks PipeConstraint and TankConstraint to show the relationship between the flow and pressure of the conserved substance (the fluid) that goes through Tank and Pipe components. [ATTACHMENT filename='two_tanks_constraints_bdd.png']
3. Tank Parametric Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the TankConstraint constraint block are bound to the properties of the Tank block (from the ****TwoTanksComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='tank_par.png']
4. Pipe Parametric Diagram : The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the PipeConstraint constraint block are bound to the properties of the Pipe block (from the TwoTanksComponents block definition diagram). [ATTACHMENT filename='pipe_par.png']
5. ConnectedTanksIBD Internal Block Diagram : This diagram is a part of the ConnectedTanks block. The interconnections between the blocks and ports are shown in this diagram. There are two part properties ( fluidReservoir1 and fluidReservoir2 ) of type Tank and a part flowMedium of type Pipe as well as ports that determine the interface between the tanks and pipe where the fluid flows through (characterized by flow rate and potential to flow in the connectors between the ports). [ATTACHMENT filename='connected_tanks_ibd.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The flow of the fluid from the tank with more fluid to the tank with less fluid is modeled in SysML v1, where it can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape for simulation. There are 5 SysML diagrams in this model:</p><ol><li><p><strong>TwoTanksComponents Block Definition Diagram</strong>: This diagram contains a block describing the <em>Tank</em> class and its <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732530 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02957: (2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models

- page_id: `272732530`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732530/2026x+Description+of+the+SignalProcessor.mdzip+SysML+v1+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:41.987+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Signal processor sample
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this model, there are three main components: an input signal generator, a signal processor, and an output signal scope. The signal flow from the input generator to the output scope is modeled by 10 SysML v1 diagrams.

1. **SignalProcessorComponents Block Definition Diagram**: This diagram contains a block describing a generic two-pin (ports) signal component class, *TwoPinSignalComponent*, that is a generalization of other blocks. The generalization contains a block describing the *Amplifier*class and its properties, a block describing the *HighPassFilter* class and its properties, and a block describing the*LowPassFilter* and its properties. The block describing the *Mixer* class (and its properties) is related to the *RealSignalInElement* and *RealSignalOutElement*interface blocks as well as *TwoPinSignalComponent* block. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. **SignalProcessorConstraints Block Definition Diagram**: Mathematical expressions are provided in constraint blocks *AmplifierConstraint*, *LowPassFilterConstraint*, *HighPassFilterConstraint* that make use of the parameters used in the constraint block *BinarySignalComponentConstraint*.These three constraint blocks, along with the *MixerConstraint* constraint block, describe the filtering and mixing of the generated signal that goes through the signal processor. The *SignalSinkConstraint*constraint block expresses the output of the signal processor as the output of the entire system, and the *SinalSourceConstraint*constraint block provides the generated input signal that flows through the system. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. **TestBed Internal Block Diagram**: This diagram is part of the *TestBed*block. The flow of signal between the part properties *inputSignal* (typed by *SignalSource*), *dSP* (typed by *SignalProcessor*), and *scopeSignalOutput*(typed by *SignalSink*) is shown to be going through ports typed by *RealSignalInElement* and *RealSignalOutElement*interface blocks. These ports are the interface between the three connected system components. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. **SignalSource Parametric Diagram**: The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the *SignalSourceConstraint* constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *SignalSource* block (from the *SignalProcessorComponents* block definition diagram). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. **SignalSink Parametric Diagram**: The constraint of the *SignalSinkConstraint* constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *SignalSink* block (from the *SignalProcessorComponents*block definition diagram). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. **SignalProcessor Internal Block Diagram**: This diagram is part of the *SignalProcessor*block and shows how the signal becomes amplified, filtered, and then remixed again. The flow of signal between the part properties *hpf* (typed by *HighPassFilter*), *lpf* (typed by*LowPassFiler*), *a* (typed by *Amplifier*), and*m* (typed by *Mixer*) is shown to be going through ports typed by *SignalPin*. These ports serve as interfaces where the signal flows from one part to the other. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. **Amplifier Parametric Diagram**: The constraints of the*AmplifierConstraint* constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *Amplifier* block (from the *SignalProcessorComponents*block definition diagram). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. **LowPassFilter Parametric Diagram**: The constraint of the *LowPassFilterConstraint* constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *LowPassFilter* block (from the *SignalProcessorComponents* block definition diagram). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. **HighPassFilter Parametric Diagram**: The constraint of the *HighPassFilterConstraint*constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *HighPassFilter* block (from the *SignalProcessorComponents* block definition diagram). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. **Mixer Parametric Diagram**: The constraint of the *MixerConstraint* constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the *Mixer*block (from the*SignalProcessorComponents* block definition diagram). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this model, there are three main components: an input signal generator, a signal processor, and an output signal scope. The signal flow from the input generator to the output scope is modeled by 10 SysML v1 diagrams.</p><ol><li><p><strong>SignalProcessorComponents Block Definition Diagram</strong>: This diagram contains a block describing a generic two-pin (ports) signal component class, <em>TwoPinSignalComponent</em>, that is a generalization of other blocks. The generalization contains a block describing the <em>Amplifier </em>class and its properties, a block describing the  <em>HighPassFilter</em> class and its properties, and a block describing the<em> LowPassFilter</em> and its properties. The block describing the <em>Mixer</em> class (and its properties) is related to the <em>RealSignalInElement</em> and <em>RealSignalOutElement </em>interface blocks as well as <em>TwoPinSignalComponent</em> block.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_processor_components_bdd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>SignalProcessorConstraints Block Definition Diagram</strong>: Mathematical expressions are provided in constraint blocks <em>AmplifierConstraint</em>, <em>LowPassFilterConstraint</em>, <em>HighPassFilterConstraint</em> that make use of the parameters used in the constraint block <em>BinarySignalComponentConstraint</em>.These three constraint blocks, along with the <em>MixerConstraint</em> constraint block, describe the filtering and mixing of the generated signal that goes through the signal processor. The <em>SignalSinkConstraint  </em>constraint block expresses the output of the signal processor as the output of the entire system, and the <em>SinalSourceConstraint  </em>constraint block provides the generated input signal that flows through the system.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_processor_constraints_bdd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>TestBed Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This diagram is part of the <em>TestBed </em>block. The flow of signal between the part properties <em>inputSignal</em> (typed by <em>SignalSource</em>), <em>dSP</em> (typed by <em>SignalProcessor</em>), and <em>scopeSignalOutput </em>(typed by  <em>SignalSink</em>) is shown to be going through ports typed by <em>RealSignalInElement</em> and <em>RealSignalOutElement </em>interface blocks. These ports are the interface between the three connected system components.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="testBed_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>SignalSource Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraint parameters (variables in the mathematical expressions of the constraints) of the <em>SignalSourceConstraint</em> constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>SignalSource</em> block (from the <em>SignalProcessorComponents</em> block definition diagram).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_source_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>SignalSink Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraint of the <em>SignalSinkConstraint</em> constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>SignalSink</em> block (from the <em>SignalProcessorComponents  </em>block definition diagram).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_sink_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>SignalProcessor Internal Block Diagram</strong>: This diagram is part of the <em>SignalProcessor  </em>block and shows how the signal becomes amplified, filtered, and then remixed again. The flow of signal between the part properties  <em>hpf</em> (typed by  <em>HighPassFilter</em>),  <em>lpf</em> (typed by<em> LowPassFiler</em>), <em>a</em> (typed by <em>Amplifier</em>), and<em> m</em> (typed by <em>Mixer</em>) is shown to be going through ports typed by <em>SignalPin</em>. These ports serve as interfaces where the signal flows from one part to the other.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_processor_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>Amplifier Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraints of the<em> AmplifierConstraint</em> constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>Amplifier</em> block (from the <em>SignalProcessorComponents  </em>block definition diagram).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="amplifier_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>LowPassFilter Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraint of the <em>LowPassFilterConstraint</em> constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>LowPassFilter</em> block (from the <em>SignalProcessorComponents</em> block definition diagram).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="low_pass_filter_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>HighPassFilter Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraint of the <em>HighPassFilterConstraint  </em>constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>HighPassFilter</em> block (from the <em>SignalProcessorComponents</em> block definition diagram).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="high_pass_filter_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML v1 models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong>Mixer Parametric Diagram</strong>: The constraint of the <em>MixerConstraint</em> constraint block are bound to the properties and ports of the <em>Mixer  </em>block (from the<em> SignalProcessorComponents</em> block definition diagram).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mixer_par.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Description of the SignalProcessor.mdzip SysML <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730288 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02958: (2026x) Diagram aspects

- page_id: `272730288`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730288/2026x+Diagram+aspects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:28.677+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['diagram-stereotype', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Diagram aspects allow you to create different views, e.g., electrical, mechanical, and optical, for the same system structure. The aspects functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allows you to use predefined aspects (only included in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML v1 modeling' space=''] or other customizations) or custom aspects for diagrams. This enables you to:

- Set any number of aspects for one diagram.
- Drill down navigation from the Part shape to its internal structure.
- Create an Aspect diagram directly from the Part shape.
- Display parts and ports according to aspects.
- Use aspects with multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout templates' space=''].

The figure below demonstrates the usage of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Finding predefined diagram aspects' space='']predefined diagram aspects. Three SysML Internal Block Diagrams of the same *Climate Control Hardware* system are of different aspects: electrical and communication. The Internal Block Diagram with the electrical aspect shows only the electrical structure of the *Climate Control Hardware* system. Correspondingly, the IBD with the communication aspect shows only the communication structure.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Climate Control Hardware views through different aspects. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

The main workflow to start using the diagrams aspect functionality in your model is the following.

To use diagram aspects

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a new project' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening projects' space=''] one.
2. Enable the diagrams aspect option. Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option>>
3. Make sure that predefined aspects are enough for you. Finding predefined diagram aspects>>
4. If predefined aspects are not what you expect, create your own aspect that you can apply to a diagram. Creating custom aspects>>
5. Create a new aspect diagram: - by creating a new diagram with already applied aspect: Creating an aspect diagram>> 
- by applying predefined (#3) or custom aspects (step #4) for an existing diagram. Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams>>
6. After your model contains diagrams with defined aspects, you can display parts and ports according to aspects and navigate among aspect diagrams.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout templates' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout templates' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying rake icon' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Profile diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model. To open it:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732927 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02959: (2026x) Diagram banners

- page_id: `272732927`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732927/2026x+Diagram+banners
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:28.689+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923016198

923016209

923016197

**On this page**

923016199

You can create diagram banners, such as a header and/or footer, to display important information on the diagram. The banners are not included in the diagram frame but are contained within the diagram pane; thus, if you choose to export the diagram ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving as image' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Printing' space='']), the banners are included. The diagram banners can be created for all diagram types, e.g. tables, matrices, or maps.

##### Creating diagram banners

To create a diagram banner

1. Right-click the diagram. In the shortcut menu, select Stereotype . The Stereotype selection menu opens.
2. In the Stereotype selection menu, select the «banner» stereotype check mark. Click the Apply button. [ATTACHMENT filename='stereotype_selection.png']
3. Right-click the diagram again and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
5. Under the «banner» stereotype, select the text tag and click the Create Value button. [ATTACHMENT filename='banner_creation.png']
6. In the Value box on the right side of the dialog, write the text that you want to have displayed on the diagram banner.
7. In the toolbar of the right-side compartment, next to the Display option, select the position for the diagram banner from the dropdown menu.
  1. Header - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top of the diagram. This is the default option.
  2. Footer - the diagram banner will be displayed at the bottom of the diagram.
  3. Both - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top and bottom of the diagram.
  4. **None** - the diagram banner will not be displayed on the diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. (Optional) To create additional banners for the diagram, click the **Add** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button and follow the steps 6-7.
9. Click **Close**. The diagram banner is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Editing diagram banners

To edit the diagram banner text and/or display position

1. Right-click the diagram and open its **Specification window**.
2. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
3. Select the **text** value under the «banner» stereotype.
4. In the Value box on the right-side compartment, select the text value you want to edit and click the Edit [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Edit the text of the banner and/or the display position via the Display option for the selected text value.
6. Click Close .

##### Removing diagram banners

To remove a diagram banner

1. Right-click the diagram and open its **Specification window**.
2. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
3. Select the **text** value under the «banner» stereotype.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To remove all banners, click the Remove Value button.
  2. To remove a single banner, in the Value box on the right-side compartment, select the text value you want to remove and click the Remove [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="866d6e0e-d37d-4df7-917d-50c0dab3be82"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923016198</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e48e154-0cd8-423d-bb47-25e98da602bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923016209</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa0c7ef0-9a5c-439e-a26f-d5b873059202"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923016197</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="536f2e85-0cff-438a-a633-cf63ce6a14e5" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cebdb548-feb1-4a97-bd5c-22aa884652c7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923016199</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create diagram banners, such as a header and/or footer, to display important information on the diagram. The banners are not included in the diagram frame but are contained within the diagram pane; thus, if you choose to export the diagram (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Saving as image" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[save it as an image]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[print it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), the banners are included. The diagram banners can be created for all diagram types, e.g. tables, matrices, or maps. </p><h3>Creating diagram banners</h3><p>To create a diagram banner</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram. In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Stereotype</strong>. The Stereotype selection menu opens.</li><li>In the Stereotype selection menu, select the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«banner»</span> stereotype check mark. Click the <strong>Apply</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stereotype_selection.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Right-click the diagram again and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li>Under the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«banner»</span> stereotype, select the <strong>text</strong> tag and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="banner_creation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> box on the right side of the dialog, write the text that you want to have displayed on the diagram banner.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the toolbar of the right-side compartment, next to the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option, select the position for the diagram banner from the dropdown menu.</span><br /><ol><li><strong>Header</strong> - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top of the diagram. This is the default option.</li><li><strong>Footer</strong> - the diagram banner will be displayed at the bottom of the diagram.</li><li><strong>Both</strong> - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top and bottom of the diagram.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>None</strong> - the diagram banner will not be displayed on the diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_position_change.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">(Optional) To create additional banners for the diagram, click the <strong>Add</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-5-24_12-25-51.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and follow the steps 6-7.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>. The diagram banner is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_banner_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Editing diagram banners</h3><p>To edit the diagram banner text and/or display position</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the diagram and open its <strong>Specification window</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Specification window, click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the <strong>text</strong> value under the «banner» stereotype.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> box on the right-side compartment, select the <strong>text</strong> value you want to edit and click the <strong>Edit</strong> <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> button.</li><li>Edit the text of the banner and/or the display position via the <strong>Display</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option</span> for the selected <strong>text</strong> value.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Removing diagram banners</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To remove a diagram banner</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the diagram and open its <strong>Specification window</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Specification window, click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the <strong>text</strong> value under the «banner» stereotype.</p></li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To remove all banners, click the <strong>Remove Value</strong> button.</li><li>To remove a single banner, in the <strong>Value</strong> box on the right-side compartment, select the <strong>text</strong> value you want to remove and click the <strong>Remove </strong><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> button.</li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738302 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02960: (2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard

- page_id: `272738302`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738302/2026x+Diagram+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:56.723+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['diagram-creation-wizard', 'cv-3-creation-wizard', 'pv-2-creation-wizard', 'acv-2-creation-wizard', 'stv-3-creation-wizard', 'npv-1-creation-wizard', 'ncv-5-creation-wizard', 'lr-lines-of-development-creation-wizard', 'cr-capability-roadmap-creation-wizard', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Diagram Creation Wizard** is used to create the following diagrams:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines' space=''] (MODAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] (MODAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships' space=''] (NAF v.3)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping' space=''] (NAF v.3)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Lr Lines of Development' space=''] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap' space=''] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Phasing diagram' space='']

The name of this wizard depends on particular diagram name: **<diagram name> Creation Wizard**:

- CV-3 Creation Wizard
- PV-2 Creation Wizard
- AcV-2 Creation Wizard
- StV-3 Creation Wizard
- NPV-1 Creation Wizard
- NCV-5 Creation Wizard
- Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard
- Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard
- Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard
- Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart Creation Wizard
- Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard

To open the **Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Diagram Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Name and owning package specification

In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, use the **Create Owner** button. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the **Clone** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability selection

In this step, you can select Capabilities from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Selects a Capability.Alternatively, double-click the Capability. |
|  | Removes a Capability. |
|  | Removes all Capabilities. |

##### The chart property setting

In this step, you can set the display style of the chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating CV-3 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating PV-2 diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732938 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02961: (2026x) Diagram frame

- page_id: `272732938`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732938/2026x+Diagram+frame
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:29.657+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568201751

568201754

568201753

All UML diagrams have the content area that is restricted by the diagram frame as it is stated in UML specification. The diagram frame is primarily used in cases where the diagrammed element has graphical border elements (like ports for classes and components, entry/exit points on state machines).

By default the diagram frame is displayed on the new diagram pane. The frame is a rectangle in all diagrams, except state machine and activity. State machine and activity diagram frames have rounded corners.

The frame can be re-sized manually by dragging its corners or borders.

To hide the diagram frame, do one of the following

- Right-click the diagram and on the shortcut menu, cancel the selection of the Show Diagram Frame .
- Open the Diagram Properties dialog and set the Show Diagram Frame property to false.

To change the diagram frame properties, do one of the following

- In the Project Options dialog > Symbol styles > Diagram > Diagram Frame options group, change the desired options.
- In the Diagram Properties dialog > Diagram Frame property group, change the properties.

To hide the icon on the diagram frame

- In the Diagram Properties dialog, change the Show Stereotypes property value to Text . [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram_frame.png']

When a new diagram is created, the diagram stereotype icon is displayed on the diagram frame header by default, though you can change it to the context stereotype icon.

To display the context stereotype icon on the diagram frame

- In the **Diagram Properties** dialog > **Diagram Frame** property group, change the **Use Stereotype** property value to **Context**. The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**. There is a possibility to show the abbreviation of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type in the diagram frame header.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- In the Diagram Properties dialog > Diagram Frame property group, select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

The abbreviated diagram types are listed in the following table.

| Diagram name | Abbreviation |
| --- | --- |
| Use Case | uc |
| Communication | comm |
| Sequence | sd |
| State Machine | stm |
| Activity | sct |
| Composite Structure | cs |

568201750

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737834 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02962: (2026x) Diagram image export

- page_id: `272737834`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737834/2026x+Diagram+image+export
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:19.438+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

3

Before exporting a diagram, you should specify the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog. You can also make additional image export changes in the**Environment Options** dialog. These options are applicable to all operations dedicated to exporting or previewing diagrams, such as:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Systems Synthesis' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents' space='MCS']

##### Specifying Project Options for diagram image export

Via the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog, you can specify the dimensions for the exported diagram, what should be done if these dimensions are exceeded, and the time it can take to build it (the latter applies only to tables and matrices). The changes you make in the **Project Options** dialog apply only to a specific project.

To specify project options for diagram image export

- In the main menu, select Options > Project > under the General group, select Diagrams > modify the options in the Image Export group.

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the diagram Image Export options and their default values.

| Project Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export diagram without the time limit.The option applies to tables and matrices only. |
| Image Height Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. |
| Image Width Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export diagram without the image width limit. |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.If the Export Preference When Limits Exceeded value is set to Export Cropped Diagram, the previewed or exported diagram is cropped based on the specified dimensions, displaying an incomplete view of the diagram that looks like the image below. |

##### Specifying Environment Options for image export

You can also make additional image export changes in the modeling tool's environment, such as image resolution and compression quality. The modifications you make in the **Environment Options** dialog persist when the application is closed and re-opened and thus apply to all projects.

To specify environment options for image export

- In the main menu, select **Options** > **Environment** > under the **General** group, select**Image Export** > modify the options in the **Image Export** group.

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the environment Image Export options and their default values.

| Environment Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Save Diagram Background in Image | false | Set to true to save the diagram together with its background as an image. Otherwise, the diagram background becomes white after saving the diagram as an image. |
| JPEG Compression Quality | 1.0 | Specify the quality for saving an image as a JPEG file. Set the value to 1.0 to define the highest quality. |
| Use SVG <text> Tag for Text Output | false | Set to true to turn all text into SVG shapes during the image conversion. |
| Letter Spacing Reduction in SVG | 3 | Specify spacing reduction between letters when exporting SVG with <text> tag. Use the slider to specify a number between 0 and 10, where 1 means -0.1 px. Increasing the number reduces the spacing between the letters. Enable the Use SVG <text> Tag for Text Output option to use the Letter Spacing Reduction in SVG option. |
| Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF | false | Set to true to render SVG icons as raster images in the EMF output. Otherwise, the SVG icons are rendered as vector graphics. |
| Image Resolution (DPI) | 72 | Specify the measure for spatial printing. |
| Exported Image Size [%] | 100 | Specify exported image scaling ratio in percent. If the set value is higher than 100, then the view is enlarged (zoomed in) before generating an image. Raster image will not lose its quality as additional pixels are introduced. |
| TIFF Color Space | RGB | Specify the color format for the tagged image file format (TIFF). |
| TIFF Compression | LZW | Specify the type for compression of the tagged image file format (TIFF). |

##### Diagram image export notifications and statistics

The following table displays the expected results of specific diagram image export options.

| Export option | Successful export | Diagram does not comply with Image Export options or the export failed |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Save as Image | All diagrams are saved as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | Single diagram export | Multiple diagrams export |
| The diagram is saved as it is in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |  |
| MDZipX Export | All diagrams are exported as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |
| MDZipX Publish | All diagrams are published as they are in the modeling tool.A notification is displayed with the information about successful publishing, e.g.: | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |

The **Details...** link in the notifications opens the **Notification Window**, which displays **Diagram Image Export Statistics**with the following information:

- Total : the number of exported diagrams.
- Cropped : the number of diagrams exported with limited dimensions if the Export Preference When Limits Exceeded option is set to Export Cropped Diagram .
- **Failed**: the number of diagrams not exported due to the exceeded building time and/or dimension limits if the **Export Preference When Limits Exceeded** option is set to *Do Not Export*. It also displays the number of diagrams whose export failed. The **Failed**and**Cropped** indications are only displayed if there are any cropped or failed diagram images. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Image export as a SVG file

Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg) format via the following options:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Systems Synthesis' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving as image' space='']

###### Disabling SVG image caching

By default, SVG images are cached in the modeling tool. However, you can disable the caching anytime by following the procedure below.

To disable SVG image caching

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located at <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dexport.svg.without.caching=true
3. Save the file and restart the modeling tool.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732941 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02963: (2026x) Diagram name and its context name synchronization

- page_id: `272732941`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732941/2026x+Diagram+name+and+its+context+name+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:29.950+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568146964

568146967

568146965

The diagram name and its context name are synchronized automatically.

Create an Activity diagram. Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:

Synchronization works in the following cases:

- Activity and Activity diagram inside.
- Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.
- (Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.
- Class and all available inner diagrams inside.

To turn off the synchronization

- In the Environment options dialog, General > Editing options group, cancel the selection of the Synchronize the diagram name with its context name option.

568146963

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']

Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:</p><p>Synchronization works in the following cases:</p><ul><li>Activity and Activity diagram inside.</li><li>Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.</li><li>(Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.</li><li>Class and all available inner diagrams inside.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To turn off the synchronization</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Environment</strong> options dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Editing</strong> options group, cancel the selection of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Synchronize the diagram name with its context name</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cf1d89c-0435-418c-b5ed-a6bc481cb496"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737844 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02964: (2026x) Diagram Overview

- page_id: `272737844`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737844/2026x+Diagram+Overview
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:19.682+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

490535609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

490535611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

490535610

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

The diagram overview functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

A diagram overview shape provides the ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Overviewing other diagrams' space=''] on a diagram pane. When using the diagram overview shape, you can overview [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tables' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Relation Map' space=''] on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of using the Diagram Overview functionality

You can format the diagram overview representation properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

To open the particular diagram's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''], double-click the diagram overview shape. The description of each diagram property appears in the same window. Descriptions are found in the description area at the bottom of the Specification window.

To create a diagram overview shape from the diagram palette

1. On the diagram palette, under the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.16.31 PM.png'] Diagram Overview button. The Select Diagram dialog opens.
2. Select the diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click OK .
3. Click the diagram pane outside the dialog. The diagram overview shape is created and shows the full content of the corresponding diagram.

490535608

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']

****

****

**Sample model**

A diagram overview sample project in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\diagram overview.mdzip*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a26ea44-842f-4fad-bde9-063de9f7ca04"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490535609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40e685e2-d77a-4afb-8e7e-7990e8b0e9e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490535611</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44f0a1a0-a8af-4de1-8232-241f1dc1a8fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490535610</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="483d9531-8e55-41b1-9ebc-97f204bacd85"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The diagram overview functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>A diagram overview shape provides the ability to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Overviewing other diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[overview other diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on a diagram pane. When using the diagram overview shape, you can overview <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency matrices]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relation Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[relation maps]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the diagram pane.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.21.22 PM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Overview" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Example of using the Diagram Overview functionality</span></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can format the diagram overview representation properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To open the particular diagram's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link>, double-click the diagram overview shape. The description of each diagram property appears in the same window. Descriptions are found in the description area at the bottom of the Specification window. </p><p><br /></p><p>To create a diagram overview shape from the diagram palette</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram palette, under the <strong>Common</strong> category, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.16.31 PM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Overview" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Diagram Overview</strong> button. The <strong>Select Diagram</strong> dialog opens.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select the diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Click the diagram pane outside the dialog. The diagram overview shape is created and shows the full content of the corresponding diagram.</li></ol><p><span><br /></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d02a4e1-5720-4e75-aa52-9dc35156305b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490535608</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="UML elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Auxiliary diagram symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Auxiliary Diagram Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>A diagram overview sample project in </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features\diagram overview.mdzip</em><span>. </span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730131 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02965: (2026x) Diagramming

- page_id: `272730131`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730131/2026x+Diagramming
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:34.269+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:

- A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.
- A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.
- Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:</p><ul><li>A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.</li><li>A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.</li><li>Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738058 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02967: (2026x) Diagrams ownership

- page_id: `272738058`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738058/2026x+Diagrams+ownership
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:27.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Organizing your model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The correct organization of your diagrams in the model ensures model consistency, reusability of required views, and quick navigation in the model.

##### SysML v1 diagram taxonomy

The figure below shows the SysML v1 diagram taxonomy, which reflects how SysML v1 diagrams are organized. You can use nine kinds of SysML diagrams when modeling:

- Requirement Diagram
- SysML Block Definition Diagram
- SysML Internal Block Diagram
- SysML Package Diagram
- SysML Parametric Diagram
- SysML Sequence Diagram
- SysML State Machine Diagram
- SysML Activity Diagram
- SysML Use Case Diagram

The relationship between SysML diagrams is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED']:

- Activity, Sequence, State Machine, and Use Case diagrams are types of behavior diagrams.
- Block Definition, Internal Block, and Package diagrams are types of structure diagrams.
- Parametric diagrams are types of Internal Block diagrams.
- Requirement diagrams are a category by themselves.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SysML diagram taxonomy.

##### Diagram owners

The owner that a diagram can represent depends on the diagram kind you are creating. All SysML diagrams and their available owners are listed in the following table.

A Behavior diagram must have a context which is always a behavior which owns this diagram.

| SysML Diagram | Owner |
| --- | --- |
|  | , , |
|  | , , , |
|  |  |
|  |  |
| SysML Package Diagram | , , |
|  | Activity |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  | , |

##### Find diagrams in your project

You can find all diagrams of your model by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagrams tab' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space='']. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagrams tab' space=''] page describes how to open and use this tab.

##### Working with diagrams

All basic procedures of working with SysML v1 diagrams can be found in the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Parametric Diagram context' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram context' space='']
