<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729780 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02968: (2026x) Diagrams tab

- page_id: `272729780`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729780/2026x+Diagrams+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:42.364+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425670349

1425670351

1425670350

INLINE

The Diagrams tab in the Model Browser represents diagrams created in your project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the **Diagrams** tab, diagrams are by default grouped by their types (see the image above).

To open the Diagrams tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Diagrams tab.
- If the Diagrams tab is hidden, from the **Window** menu, select **Diagrams**. You can change some Diagrams tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Diagrams tab consists of the following two components:

- Diagrams tab toolbar
- Diagrams tree

##### Diagrams tab toolbar

The Diagrams tab toolbar contains the following button:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only the selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |

Some of these actions may be performed right in the shortcut menu of the Diagrams tree.

##### Working with model elements in the Diagrams tree

You can work with diagrams in the Diagrams tree using the commands available in the selected diagram's shortcut menu. These are the commonly used commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Open | Opens the selected diagram. |
| Print | Opens the Print dialog. Active for the open diagram only. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |

1425670348

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space='']

Diagrams tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Group by Diagram Name</p></td><td><p>Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagrams tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing <br />to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites</li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagrams tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>Some of these actions may be performed right in the shortcut menu of the Diagrams tree.</p><h3>Working with model elements in the Diagrams tree</h3><p>You can work with diagrams in the Diagrams tree using the commands available in the selected diagram's shortcut menu. These are the commonly used commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 55.4439%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 26.0081%;" /><col style="width: 73.9919%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the selected diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Print</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>O<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f">pens the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f">Print</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f"> dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7875c34d-efa0-411c-a90e-bb3100d636cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Active for the open diagram only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f">Select in Containment</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f"> </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f">Tree</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Group by Diagram Name</strong></td><td colspan="1">Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737955 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02969: (2026x) Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area

- page_id: `272737955`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737955/2026x+Dialog+for+customizing+selected+modeling+tool+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:26.187+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The **Customize <Area>** dialog enables you to customize your own environment, from simple to complex or vice versa. You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.

The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is **Customize Main Menu**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:

- Standard and Expert . The selected element will be visible in the Standard as well as in the Expert mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.
- Expert . The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.
- Hidden . The selected element will not be shown in any mode.

To assign an element to the mode

1. Select an element.
2. Click the radio button under the desired mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.</p><p>The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is <strong>Customize Main Menu</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Customize Main Menu" ac:alt="Customize Main Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize_main_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Standard and Expert</strong>. The selected element will be visible in the <strong>Standard</strong> as well as in the <strong>Expert</strong> mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.</li><li><strong>Expert</strong>. The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.</li><li><strong>Hidden</strong>. The selected element will not be shown in any mode.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To assign an element to the mode</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Click the radio button under the desired mode.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735398 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02970: (2026x) Dialog tool

- page_id: `272735398`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735398/2026x+Dialog+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:20.674+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358524304

1358524305

1358524305

The purpose of a Dialog tool is to enable report templates to call functions for creating dialogs to interact with users during the report generation process. To modify the report templates, you can use the Dialog tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Class Diagram: DialogTool.

The Dialog tool uses the following methods:

- message This method will create message dialogs.

- confirm This method will create confirmation dialogs and return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click the OK button or return ‘false’ when you click the Cancel button.

- input This method will create input dialogs and return the input value as a string.

- **sort** 
This method will create Sort and Enable dialogs and return a collection of newly-sorted results.

- **select** 
This method will create a Selection dialog and return a collection of the selected elements in the dialog.

Like other Custom tools, the Dialog tool (*dialogtool.jar*) must be installed in the ‘extensions’ folder of the Report Wizard plugin.

To import a Dialog tool to a template, type the following:

```text

```

1358524303

**Related page**

## PAGE 02971: (2026x) Dialog tool API

- page_id: `272738871`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738871/2026x+Dialog+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:44.129+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Dialog tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

815916921

815916923

815916919

32

815916922

#### ‘message’ Method

**message(String message) : void**. This method will create a **Message** dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Message dialog.

#### ‘confirm’ Method

**confirm(String message) : boolean**. This method will create a **Confirmation** dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘false’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Confirmation dialog.

#### ‘input’ Method

##### Input dialog with text

**input(String message) : String**. This method will create an **Input** dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value

**input(String message, String initialValue)** : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and an initial value.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value Array

**input(String message, Collection options)** : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.

#### ‘sort’ Method

##### Sort and Enable dialog

**sort(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with text

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog with text.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option

**sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title

**sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

#### 'select' Method

##### Default Selection dialog

**select(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the **Selection** dialog. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog.

##### Selection dialog with a title

**select(Collection list, String title) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog with a specified dialog title. The default **Selection** dialog title is **Selection**. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with title.

##### Selection dialog without selection

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Selection dialog without selection.

##### Selection dialog with description text

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with text.

##### Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c004677-7e7f-4d5c-ad0f-6041d127cb3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916921</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62fc515f-d9cd-4ffc-b71e-c3c344e852a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae66343e-dc33-476f-8447-53f654f95b19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5788a78-023d-417a-bb7d-7e98e39b332d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91e9ae09-de8b-4f61-a45a-8446283c85cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916922</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>‘message’ Method</h2><p><strong>message(String message) : void</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Message</strong> dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b8245b9-9013-4a69-acbe-cafa02079d8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$dialog.message("Click OK to close dialog")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 1- Message Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Message dialog.</h6><h2>‘confirm’ Method</h2><p><strong>confirm(String message) : boolean</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Confirmation</strong> dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘false’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e588332-753e-421d-838c-29368f7edbbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($dialog.confirm("Do you want to generate section A?"))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 2- Confirmation Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Confirmation dialog.</h6><h2>‘input’ Method</h2><h3>Input dialog with text</h3><p><strong>input(String message) : String</strong>. This method will create an <strong>Input</strong> dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e8e5c24-a4eb-4a58-8b7e-e7600c0d1129"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter your name"))
#if ($userText)
Your name is $userText
#else
Please specify your name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 3-Input Dialog with Text.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value</h3><p><strong>input(String message, String initialValue)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac29d2bc-4673-44ee-a29b-c0e2e2c305f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter a date", "Sep28,2016"))
Date is $userText]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4- The Input Dialog with Text and an Initial Value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and an initial value.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value Array</h3><p><strong>input(String message, Collection options)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f13fca1d-36c0-41e2-8943-88c619afc6a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($selectedOption = $dialog.input("Choose your favorite fruit", ["Apple", "Orange", "Banana"]))
Your favorite fruit is $selectedOption]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 5- Input Dialog with Text and Initial Value Array.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.</h6><h2>‘sort’ Method</h2><h3>Sort and Enable dialog</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64ff03e1-5242-4f63-867c-a137bab037fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 6- Sort and Enable Dialog..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with text</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09fb2a92-95c1-4689-aba8-e1bcf52457b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort("Rearrange diagram for presentation report",$Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 7- Sort and Enable Dialog with Text..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog with text.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><br /></p><h2>'select' Method</h2><h3>Default Selection dialog</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the <strong>Selection</strong> dialog. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e72fea-9047-4630-89f9-318454f58595"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $dialog.select($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with a title</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog with a specified dialog title. The default <strong>Selection</strong> dialog title is <strong>Selection</strong>. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aea59cd0-54b8-4b8a-bd4f-9a742dbdef27"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection Dialog"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog with title.</h6><h3>Selection dialog without selection</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef295ca9-3dd5-4f57-8a06-269337e8a62b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection unchecked Dialog", false))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Unchecked.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> The Selection dialog without selection.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with description text</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="283f7f60-238c-446a-ac2f-04dce126994d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram,“Select all feature”, false, "Please select your required element"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select All.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog with text.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732779 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02973: (2026x) Direction prefixes

- page_id: `272732779`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732779/2026x+Direction+prefixes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:14.280+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x) Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display and hide the direction prefixes. They help to identify directions of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']. Read the following sections to learn how to work with direction prefixes:

33

##### Displaying direction prefixes of Proxy Port

The direction prefix of the Proxy Port helps to identify its direction.

The direction prefixes of Proxy Ports are displayed:

- 
- 
- 
- 

###### compIn Block Compartment

To display the Proxy Port direction prefixes in the Block Compartment

1. Right-click the Block and select the **Symbol Properties**.
2. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Proxy Port Direction in Compartment** property value to *true*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### browserIn the Model Browser

The direction prefixes of the Proxy Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### proxy_portOn the Proxy Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape

To display the direction prefix on the Proxy Port shape

1. Right-click the Proxy Port shapeand select **Position of Labels** > **Name and Type Labels Inside** or**All Labels Inside**.
2. Right-click the Proxy Port shape and select the **Symbol Properties**.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Direction Prefix Inside Port** property value to *true*. 
The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Proxy Port shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='direction_prefix_on_proxy_port_shape.png']

###### tooltipOn the ToolTip

The direction prefixes of the Proxy Port on the ToolTip are displayed automatically. To see it, move the pointer over the Proxy Port or its name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying combined direction on Proxy Port shape

Combined direction consists of all owned and inherited flow properties and directed features of the Proxy Port. The flow properties direction are shown by default on the Proxy Port shape. If all features have direction "out" or "provided", the combined direction is "out". If all features have direction "in" or"required", the combined direction is "in". Otherwise the direction is "inout".

To include directed features into combined direction of the Proxy Port

1. From the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the**Project Options** dialog, on the right side, click **General** > **SysML**.
3. Set the **Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port** property value to *true*. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding direction prefixes of Flow Property on Part Property shape

To hide direction prefix of Flow property on Part Property shape

1. Right-click the Part property shape and select **Symbol Properties**.
2. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Flow Property Direction in Compartment** property value to *false*.

##### Displaying direction prefixes of Full Port

The direction prefix of the Full Port helps to identify its direction.

The direction prefixes of FullPorts are displayed:

- 
- On the FullPort shape
- 

###### modIn the Model Browser

The direction prefixes of the Full Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### fullOn the Full Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape

To display the direction prefix on the Full Port shape

1. Right-click the Full Port shapeand select **Position of Labels** > **Name and Type Labels Inside** or**All Labels Inside**.
2. Right-click the Full Port shape and select the **Symbol Properties**.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Direction Prefix Inside Port** property value to *true*. 
The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Full Port shape. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### tipOn the ToolTip

The direction prefixes of the Full Port on the ToolTip are displayed automatically. To see it, move the pointer over the Full Port or its name. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="">tooltip</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the ToolTip</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of the Proxy Port on the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ToolTip</span> are displayed automatically.</span> To see it, move the pointer over the Proxy Port or its name.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="direction_prefix_on_tooltip.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Direction prefixes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Displaying combined direction on Proxy Port shape</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Combined direction consists of all owned and inherited flow properties and directed features of the Proxy Port. The flow properties direction are shown by default on the Proxy Port shape. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If all features have direction &quot;out&quot; or &quot;provided&quot;, the combined direction is &quot;out&quot;. If all features have direction &quot;in&quot; or&quot;required&quot;, the combined direction is &quot;in&quot;. Otherwise the direction is &quot;inout&quot;.</span></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To include directed features into combined direction of the Proxy Port</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, on the right side, click <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Set the <strong>Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="combined_direction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Direction prefixes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><h3>Hiding direction prefixes of Flow Property on Part Property shape</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To hide direction prefix of Flow property on Part Property shape</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Part property shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Flow Property Direction in Compartment</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Displaying direction prefixes of Full Port</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefix of the Full Port helps to identify its direction.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Full </span>Ports are displayed:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="mod"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="full"><ac:link-body>On the Full<span class="confluence-link"> </span>Port shape</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="tip"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[On the ToolTip]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></li></ul><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a32ba11-1bb2-4d47-8988-4fc482ab8f20"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mod</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>In the Model Browser</span></span></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of the Full Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="full_port_direction_prefix_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Direction prefixes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="516f5eb7-08b7-41d7-8ce1-dde84a0c07cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">full</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the Full Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display the direction prefix on the Full Port shape</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Full <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Port shape</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> and select</span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Position of Labels</strong></span> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Name and Type Labels Inside</strong> or<strong> All Labels Inside</strong></span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Full <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Port shape and</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">select <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Show Direction Prefix Inside Port</strong> property value to <em>true</em></span>.<br />The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Full Port shape.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="direction_prefix_on_full_port_shape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Direction prefixes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e182e7f4-df59-436e-9170-fac5a3b3bdfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">tip</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the ToolTip</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of the Full Port on the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ToolTip</span> are displayed automatically.</span> To see it, move the pointer over the Full Port or its name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="full_port_direction_prefix_on_tooltip.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Direction prefixes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739813 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02974: (2026x) Disabling inbound network activity

- page_id: `272739813`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739813/2026x+Disabling+inbound+network+activity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:00.223+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Useful information

INLINE

Note that after disabling inbound network activity, you will not be able to open the modeling tool Help or submit an issue.

#### TIP: Useful information

Useful information

If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.

To disable inbound network activity

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the properties file, e.g., <modeling tool name>.properties .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:
3. Save and close the file.

issue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4602c53-adae-4ca0-9514-aa04dc3c211f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To disable inbound network activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731307 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02975: (2026x) Display all sub capabilities in NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `272731307`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731307/2026x+Display+all+sub+capabilities+in+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:44.342+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731309 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02976: (2026x) Display sub capabilities removed from NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `272731309`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731309/2026x+Display+sub+capabilities+removed+from+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:44.537+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display only the selected sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all sub capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bd530ef-d938-4850-90ee-b4f73791cab8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display only the selected sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>Select All</strong> to display all sub capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732875 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02977: (2026x) Displaying actual Connectors and ports

- page_id: `272732875`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732875/2026x+Displaying+actual+Connectors+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:39.190+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram > (2026x) Implied Connectors
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to see the detailed view underneath the implied relation, you can display actual Connectors and ports directly from the implied relation in the following ways:

- Using the Semantic Zoom In button to show actual ports and Connectors by expanding only one level into deep at a time. How to use the Semantic Zoom >>
- Using the Display Underlying Connectors command to show all actual ports and Connectors at once. How to use **Display Underlying Connectors** command >>

##### Using the Semantic Zoom

This feature allows you to view details of the implied relation and display nested ports level by level into deep. The **Semantic Zoom****In** button displays ports and/or Connectors which are underneath the implied relation by expanding only one level into deep at a time. You can use that button until all actual ports and Connectors are displayed and implied relation disappears. The **Semantic Zoom****Out** button collapses displayed actual ports and/or Connectors on the diagram one level at a time.

To display/collapse actual ports and Connectors one level into deep at a time

1. Select the implied relation.
2. From its smart manipulator toolbar select: 
- The**Semantic Zoom In** button ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) to display actual ports and Connectors one level into deep. 
- The**Semantic Zoom Out** button ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) to collapse displayed actual ports and Connectors one level into deep.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying/collapsing all actual ports and Connectors by using the Semantic Zoom In/Out button.

##### Using the Display Underlying Connectors command

You can display all actual Connectors and ports from implied relations at once with one click.

To display all actual Connectors and ports at once

1. Select the implied relation.
2. From its shortcut menu, select the Display Underlying Connectors command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying all actual Connectors and ports by using the Display Underlying Connectors command.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Property path notation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

1421694496

1421694494

You can customize the content of the **Merged Result** tree/list as well as navigate through changes in both the **Merged Results** tree/list and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Merged** **Results** tree/list and navigating through changes are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Accept the selected change and go to the next change | Click to accept the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to accept the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to accept all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model selected, then all changes for the whole project will be accepted. |
|  | Reject the selected change and go to the next change | Click to reject the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to reject the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to reject all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model is selected, then all changes for the whole project will be rejected. |
| Alt + Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change.The button is not available if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt + Up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + Down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt + End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available if the last change is already selected. |
| Alt + Page Down | Go To Next Conflict | Click to select the next conflict. The button is not available if there are no conflicts. |
|  | Annotate Merged Diagram | Click to turn on showing merged diagram annotations. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a Project Merge differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| Options menu |  |  |
| Icon | Option | Description |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog. Clear the checkbox next to an element that you want to hide in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| > | Show Elements from Used projects | Click to show or hide elements from used projects in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| Show Source | Click to show or hide Source column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Ancestor | Click to show or hide Ancestor column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is hidden. |  |
| Show Target | Click to show or hide Target column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Merge Result Preview | Click to show or hide Show Merge Result Preview column in the Specification panel. This column shows final changes in the merged project and by default, this column is shown. |  |
| Summary/Legend | Click to show or hide the Summary/Legend panel. |  |
| Reset Windows Configuration | Select to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |  |

1421694492

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739584 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02979: (2026x) Displaying and navigating through differences

- page_id: `272739584`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739584/2026x+Displaying+and+navigating+through+differences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:33.846+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Comparing projects > (2026x) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'comparison-result-navigation', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the content of the **Differences** tree as well as navigate through differences in both the **Difference**tree and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Difference Viewer**window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Differences** tree and navigating through differences are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand | Click to expand all nodes in the Differences tree. |
|  | Collapse | Click to collapse all nodes in the Differences tree. |
| Alt+Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change. The button is not available, if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt+ up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt+End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available, if the last change is already selected. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a project comparison differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Items Filter dialog. Clear the check-box next to an element that you need to hide in the Differences tree. |
| > | Reset Windows Configuration | Click to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |

You can also filter the elements displayed in the **Differences** tree by change type. Use the **Display** drop-down list to specify a change type. Be advised that filtering elements by a particular change type displays both elements with the particular changes and elements with the particular owned element changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734199 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02980: (2026x) Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes

- page_id: `272734199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734199/2026x+Displaying+and+suppressing+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:29.231+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Compartments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click the shape and click the *Compartments* smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations. The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- On the diagram, select a shape and click the Suppress <compartment name> smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='supress_attributes.png']
- On the diagram, right-click the shape and select Symbol Properties . Then select or cancel the selection of the Suppress <compartment name> check box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the diagram, click the shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9656a37f-5122-411f-9ed7-4b5acae41ad2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the diagram, select a shape and click the <em>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</em> smart manipulator. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="supress_attributes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the diagram, right-click the shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. Then select or cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress </strong>&lt;compartment name&gt; check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736118 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02981: (2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements

- page_id: `272736118`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736118/2026x+Displaying+Custom+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:42.765+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Measurements > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['add-measurement', 'delete-measurement', 'remove-measurement', 'clear-all', 'display-custom-measurements', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the <viewpoint name>* Connectivity and Role-based Connectivity tables by . Measurements can be displayed for the <viewpoint specific>** Exchanges. 
When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Measurements dialog' space='']**Measurements** dialog. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.

You can manage custom Measurements byremoving them. 
 
addTo add a column with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the**Measurements** dialog, click the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange or <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item tab .
3. In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

removeTo remove columns with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the **Measurements**dialog, click the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange or <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item tab .
3. In the opened tab, click the **Clear** **All** button to clear all the selected check boxes.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following actions:

- Removing the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed <viewpoint specific>** Exchange is used by the other <viewpoint specific>** Exchange in the table.
- Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item in the table.
- Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**** <viewpoint specific>** - Operational, Resources, Services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735833 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02982: (2026x) Displaying dependency details

- page_id: `272735833`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735833/2026x+Displaying+dependency+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:08.811+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Model Visualizer > (2026x) Package Dependency Diagram Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package diagram' space=''], the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the **Virtual dependencies** package, which you can find in the model browser.

You can use the **Show Dependency Details** command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display the dependency details

1. In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.
2. In the shortcut menu, select Related Elements > Show Dependency Details . The dependency details panel opens.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package diagram" /></ac:link>, the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the <strong>Virtual dependencies</strong> package, which you can find in the model browser.</p><p>You can use the <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong> command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Package_diagram_dep.details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying dependency details" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To display the dependency details</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b51d8020-1fa1-4c88-b08f-668798c33b7a">In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.</li><li data-uuid="07f7a5ba-e3ee-466d-adb8-e6cda7dbd92d">In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong>. The dependency details panel opens.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="37ec7a24-f373-4dc1-b290-832e6a90c410"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="93ec554a-b3d5-4066-bf7f-bd6736165f1e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
